Mark6:26 The king deeply regretted his promise. But because of his oath and his guests, he didn't want to refuse her." 
Mark6:27 Immediately, the king sent a guard and ordered him to bring John's head. The guard cut off John's head in prison." 
Mark6:27 Immediately, the king sent a guard and ordered him to bring John's head. The guard cut off John's head in prison." 
Mark6:28 Then he brought the head on a platter and gave it to the girl, and the girl gave it to her mother." 
Mark6:29 When John's disciples heard about this, they came for his body and laid it in a tomb." 
Mark6:29 When John's disciples heard about this, they came for his body and laid it in a tomb." 
Mark6:30 The apostles gathered around Jesus. They reported to him everything they had done and taught. 
Mark6:31 So he said to them, ""Let's go to a place where we can be alone to rest for a while."" Many people were coming and going, and Jesus and the apostles didn't even have a chance to eat." 
Mark6:32 So they went away in a boat to a place where they could be alone. 
Mark6:33 But many people saw them leave and recognized them. The people ran from all the cities and arrived ahead of them. 
Mark6:34 When Jesus got out of the boat, he saw a large crowd and felt sorry for them. They were like sheep without a shepherd. So he spent a lot of time teaching them." 
Mark6:35 When it was late, his disciples came to him. They said, ""No one lives around here, and it's already late." 
Mark6:36 Send the people to the closest farms and villages to buy themselves something to eat. 
Mark6:37 Jesus replied, ""You give them something to eat."" They said to him, ""Should we go and spend about a year's wages on bread to feed them?""" 
Mark6:38 He said to them, ""How many loaves do you have? Go and see."" When they found out, they told him, ""Five loaves of bread and two fish.""" 
Mark6:39 Then he ordered all of them to sit down in groups on the green grass. 
Mark6:40 They sat down in groups of hundreds and fifties. 
Mark6:41 After he took the five loaves and the two fish, he looked up to heaven and blessed the food. He broke the loaves apart and kept giving them to the disciples to give to the people. He also gave pieces of the two fish to everyone." 
Mark6:42 All of them ate as much as they wanted. 
Mark6:43 When they picked up the leftover pieces, they filled twelve baskets with bread and fish." 
Mark6:44 There were 5,000 men who had eaten the bread." 
Mark6:45 Jesus quickly made his disciples get into a boat and cross to Bethsaida ahead of him while he sent the people away. 
Mark6:46 After saying goodbye to them, he went up a mountain to pray." 
Mark6:47 When evening came, the boat was in the middle of the sea, and he was alone on the land." 
Mark6:48 Jesus saw that they were in a lot of trouble as they rowed, because they were going against the wind. Between three and six o'clock in the morning, he came to them. He was walking on the sea. He wanted to pass by them." 
Mark6:49 When they saw him walking on the sea, they thought, ""It's a ghost!"" and they began to scream." 
Mark6:50 All of them saw him and were terrified. Immediately, he said, ""Calm down! It's me. Don't be afraid!""" 
Mark6:51 He got into the boat with them, and the wind stopped blowing. The disciples were astounded." 
Mark6:52 (They didn't understand what had happened with the loaves of bread. Instead, their minds were closed.)" 
Mark6:53 They crossed the sea, came to shore at Gennesaret, and anchored there." 
Mark6:54 As soon as they stepped out of the boat, the people recognized Jesus." 
Mark6:55 They ran all over the countryside and began to carry the sick on cots to any place where they heard he was. 
Mark6:56 Whenever he would go into villages, cities, or farms, people would put their sick in the marketplaces. They begged him to let them touch the edge of his clothes. Everyone who touched his clothes was made well." 
Mark7:1 The Pharisees and some scribes who had come from Jerusalem gathered around Jesus. 
Mark7:2 They saw that some of his disciples were unclean because they ate without washing their hands. 
Mark7:3 (The Pharisees, like all other Jewish people, don't eat unless they have properly washed their hands. They follow the traditions of their ancestors." 
Mark7:4 When they come from the marketplace, they don't eat unless they have washed first. They have been taught to follow many other rules. For example, they must also wash their cups, jars, brass pots, and dinner tables.)" 
Mark7:5 The Pharisees and the scribes asked Jesus, ""Why don't your disciples follow the traditions taught by our ancestors? They are unclean because they don't wash their hands before they eat!""" 
Mark7:6 Jesus told them, ""Isaiah was right when he prophesied about you hypocrites in Scripture: 'These people honor me with their lips, but their hearts are far from me." 
Mark7:7 Their worship of me is pointless, because their teachings are rules made by humans.'" 
Mark7:8 You abandon the commandments of God to follow human traditions. 
Mark7:9 He added, ""You have no trouble rejecting the commandments of God in order to keep your own traditions!" 
Mark7:10 For example, Moses said, 'Honor your father and your mother' and 'Whoever curses father or mother must be put to death.'" 
Mark7:11 But you say, 'If a person tells his father or mother that whatever he might have used to help them is corban (that is, an offering to God)," 
Mark7:12 he no longer has to do anything for his father or mother.' 
Mark7:13 Because of your traditions you have destroyed the authority of God's word. And you do many other things like that. 
Mark7:14 Then he called the crowd again and said to them, ""Listen to me, all of you, and try to understand!" 
Mark7:15 Nothing that goes into a person from the outside can make him unclean. It's what comes out of a person that makes him unclean. 
Mark7:16 Let the person who has ears listen! 
Mark7:17 When he had left the people and gone home, his disciples asked him about this illustration." 
Mark7:18 Jesus said to them, ""Don't you understand? Don't you know that whatever goes into a person from the outside can't make him unclean?" 
Mark7:19 It doesn't go into his thoughts but into his stomach and then into a toilet. (By saying this, Jesus declared all foods acceptable.)" 
Mark7:20 He continued, ""It's what comes out of a person that makes him unclean." 
Mark7:21 Evil thoughts, sexual sins, stealing, murder," 
Mark7:22 adultery, greed, wickedness, cheating, shameless lust, envy, cursing, arrogance, and foolishness come from within a person." 
Mark7:23 All these evils come from within and make a person unclean. 
Mark7:24 Jesus left that place and went to the territory of Tyre. He didn't want anyone to know that he was staying in a house there. However, it couldn't be kept a secret." 
Mark7:25 A woman whose little daughter had an evil spirit heard about Jesus. She went to him and bowed down. 
Mark7:26 The woman happened to be Greek, born in Phoenicia in Syria. She asked him to force the demon out of her daughter." 
Mark7:27 Jesus said to her, ""First, let the children eat all they want. It's not right to take the children's food and throw it to the dogs.""" 
Mark7:28 She answered him, ""Lord, even the dogs under the table eat some of the children's scraps.""" 
Mark7:29 Jesus said to her, ""Because you have said this, go! The demon has left your daughter.""" 
Mark7:30 The woman went home and found the little child lying on her bed, and the demon was gone." 
Mark7:31 Jesus then left the neighborhood of Tyre. He went through Sidon and the territory of the Ten Cities to the Sea of Galilee. 
Mark7:32 Some people brought to him a man who was deaf and who also had a speech defect. They begged Jesus to lay his hand on him. 
Mark7:33 Jesus took him away from the crowd to be alone with him. He put his fingers into the man's ears, and after spitting, he touched the man's tongue." 
Mark7:34 Then he looked up to heaven, sighed, and said to the man, ""Ephphatha!"" which means, ""Be opened!""" 
Mark7:35 At once the man could hear and talk normally. 
Mark7:36 Jesus ordered the people not to tell anyone. But the more he ordered them, the more they spread the news." 
Mark7:37 Jesus completely amazed the people. They said, ""He has done everything well. He makes the deaf hear and the mute talk.""" 
Mark8:1 About that time there was once again a large crowd with nothing to eat. Jesus called his disciples and said to them," 
Mark8:2 I feel sorry for the people. They have been with me three days now and have nothing to eat. 
Mark8:3 If I send them home before they've eaten, they will become exhausted on the road. Some of them have come a long distance.""" 
Mark8:4 His disciples asked him, ""Where could anyone get enough bread to feed these people in this place where no one lives?""" 
Mark8:5 Jesus asked them, ""How many loaves of bread do you have?"" They answered, ""Seven.""" 
Mark8:6 He ordered the crowd to sit down on the ground. He took the seven loaves and gave thanks to God. Then he broke the bread and gave it to his disciples to serve to the people. 
Mark8:7 They also had a few small fish. He blessed them and said that the fish should also be served to the people. 
Mark8:8 The people ate as much as they wanted. The disciples picked up the leftover pieces and filled seven large baskets. 
Mark8:9 About four thousand people were there. Then he sent the people on their way. 
Mark8:10 After that, Jesus and his disciples got into a boat and went into the region of Dalmanutha." 
Mark8:11 The Pharisees went to Jesus and began to argue with him. They tested him by demanding that he perform a miraculous sign from heaven. 
Mark8:12 With a deep sigh he asked, ""Why do these people demand a sign? I can guarantee this truth: If these people are given a sign, it will be far different than what they want!""" 
Mark8:13 Then he left them there. He got into a boat again and crossed to the other side of the Sea of Galilee. 
Mark8:14 The disciples had forgotten to take any bread along and had only one loaf with them in the boat. 
Mark8:15 Jesus warned them, ""Be careful! Watch out for the yeast of the Pharisees and the yeast of Herod!""" 
Mark8:16 They had been discussing with one another that they didn't have any bread. 
Mark8:17 Jesus knew what they were saying and asked them, ""Why are you discussing the fact that you don't have any bread? Don't you understand yet? Don't you catch on? Are your minds closed?" 
Mark8:18 Are you blind and deaf? Don't you remember? 
Mark8:19 When I broke the five loaves for the five thousand, how many baskets did you fill with leftover pieces?"" They told him, ""Twelve.""" 
Mark8:20 When I broke the seven loaves for the four thousand, how many large baskets did you fill with leftover pieces?"" They answered him, ""Seven.""" 
Mark8:21 He asked them, ""Don't you catch on yet?""" 
Mark8:22 As they came to Bethsaida, some people brought a blind man to Jesus. They begged Jesus to touch him." 
Mark8:23 Jesus took the blind man's hand and led him out of the village. He spit into the man's eyes and placed his hands on him. Jesus asked him, ""Can you see anything?""" 
Mark8:24 The man looked up and said, ""I see people. They look like trees walking around.""" 
Mark8:25 Then Jesus placed his hands on the man's eyes a second time, and the man saw clearly. His sight was normal again. He could see everything clearly even at a distance." 
Mark8:26 Jesus told him when he sent him home, ""Don't go into the village.""" 
Mark8:27 Then Jesus and his disciples went to the villages around Caesarea Philippi. On the way he asked his disciples, ""Who do people say I am?""" 
Mark8:28 They answered him, ""Some say you are John the Baptizer, others Elijah, still others one of the prophets.""" 
Mark8:28 They answered him, ""Some say you are John the Baptizer, others Elijah, still others one of the prophets.""" 
Mark8:29 He asked them, ""But who do you say I am?"" Peter answered him, ""You are the Messiah!""" 
Mark8:30 He ordered them not to tell anyone about him. 
Mark8:31 Then he began to teach them that the Son of Man would have to suffer a lot. He taught them that he would be rejected by the leaders, the chief priests, and the scribes. He would be killed, but after three days he would come back to life." 
Mark8:32 He told them very clearly what he meant. Peter took him aside and objected to this. 
Mark8:33 Jesus turned, looked at his disciples, and objected to what Peter said. Jesus said, ""Get out of my way, Satan! You aren't thinking the way God thinks but the way humans think.""" 
Mark8:34 Then Jesus called the crowd to himself along with his disciples. He said to them, ""Those who want to follow me must say no to the things they want, pick up their crosses, and follow me." 
Mark8:35 Those who want to save their lives will lose them. But those who lose their lives for me and for the Good News will save them. 
Mark8:36 What good does it do for people to win the whole world yet lose their lives? 
Mark8:37 Or what should a person give in exchange for life? 
Mark8:38 If people are ashamed of me and what I say in this unfaithful and sinful generation, the Son of Man will be ashamed of those people when he comes with the holy angels in his Father's glory."""

Mark9:16 Jesus asked the scribes, ""What are you arguing about with them?""" 
Mark9:17 A man in the crowd answered, ""Teacher, I brought you my son. He is possessed by a spirit that won't let him talk." 
Mark9:18 Whenever the spirit brings on a seizure, it throws him to the ground. Then he foams at the mouth, grinds his teeth, and becomes exhausted. I asked your disciples to force the spirit out, but they didn't have the power to do it.""" 
Mark9:19 Jesus said to them, ""You unbelieving generation! How long must I be with you? How long must I put up with you? Bring him to me!""" 
Mark9:20 They brought the boy to him. As soon as the spirit saw Jesus, it threw the boy into convulsions. He fell on the ground, rolled around, and foamed at the mouth." 
Mark9:21 Jesus asked his father, ""How long has he been like this?"" The father replied, ""He has been this way since he was a child." 
Mark9:22 The demon has often thrown him into fire or into water to destroy him. If it's possible for you, put yourself in our place, and help us!""" 
Mark9:23 Jesus said to him, ""As far as possibilities go, everything is possible for the person who believes.""" 
Mark9:24 The child's father cried out at once, ""I believe! Help my lack of faith.""" 
Mark9:25 When Jesus saw that a crowd was running to the scene, he gave an order to the evil spirit. He said, ""You spirit that won't let him talk, I command you to come out of him and never enter him again."""

Mark9:27 Jesus took his hand and helped him to stand up. 
Mark9:28 When Jesus went into a house, his disciples asked him privately, ""Why couldn't we force the spirit out of the boy?""" 
Mark9:29 He told them, ""This kind of spirit can be forced out only by prayer.""" 
Mark9:30 They left that place and were passing through Galilee. Jesus did not want anyone to know where he was 
Mark9:31 because he was teaching his disciples. He taught them, ""The Son of Man will be betrayed and handed over to people. They will kill him, but on the third day he will come back to life.""" 
Mark9:32 The disciples didn't understand what he meant and were afraid to ask him. 
Mark9:33 Then they came to Capernaum. While Jesus was at home, he asked the disciples, ""What were you arguing about on the road?""" 
Mark9:34 They were silent. On the road they had argued about who was the greatest. 
Mark9:35 He sat down and called the twelve apostles. He told them, ""Whoever wants to be the most important person must take the last place and be a servant to everyone else.""" 
Mark9:36 Then he took a little child and had him stand among them. He put his arms around the child and said to them,"

Mark9:38 John said to Jesus, ""Teacher, we saw someone forcing demons out of a person by using the power and authority of your name. We tried to stop him because he was not one of us."""

Mark9:39 Jesus said, ""Don't stop him! No one who works a miracle in my name can turn around and speak evil of me." 
Mark9:40 Whoever isn't against us is for us. 
Mark9:41 I can guarantee this truth: Whoever gives you a cup of water to drink because you belong to Christ will certainly not lose his reward. 
Mark9:42 These little ones believe in me. It would be best for the person who causes one of them to lose faith to be thrown into the sea with a large stone hung around his neck. 
Mark9:43 So if your hand causes you to lose your faith, cut it off! It is better for you to enter life disabled than to have two hands and go to hell, to the fire that cannot be put out."

Mark10:16 Jesus put his arms around the children and blessed them by placing his hands on them.

Mark10:19 You know the commandments: Never murder. Never commit adultery. Never steal. Never give false testimony. Never cheat. Honor your father and mother.

Mark10:31 But many who are first will be last, and the last will be first."""

Mark11:23 I can guarantee this truth: This is what will be done for someone who doesn't doubt but believes what he says will happen: He can say to this mountain, 'Be uprooted and thrown into the sea,' and it will be done for him." 
Mark11:24 That's why I tell you to have faith that you have already received whatever you pray for, and it will be yours." 
Mark11:25 Whenever you pray, forgive anything you have against anyone. Then your Father in heaven will forgive your failures."""

Mark12:1 Then, using this illustration, Jesus spoke to them. He said, ""A man planted a vineyard. He put a wall around it, made a vat for the winepress, and built a watchtower. Then he leased it to vineyard workers and went on a trip." 
Mark12:2 At the right time he sent a servant to the workers to collect from them a share of the grapes from the vineyard. 
Mark12:3 The workers took the servant, beat him, and sent him back with nothing." 
Mark12:4 So the man sent another servant to them. They hit the servant on the head and treated him shamefully. 
Mark12:5 The man sent another, and they killed that servant. Then he sent many other servants. Some of these they beat, and others they killed." 
Mark12:6 He had one more person to send. That person was his son, whom he loved. Finally, he sent his son to them. He thought, 'They will respect my son.'" 
Mark12:7 But those workers said to one another, 'This is the heir. Let's kill him, and the inheritance will be ours.'" 
Mark12:8 So they took him, killed him, and threw him out of the vineyard." 
Mark12:9 What will the owner of the vineyard do? He will come and destroy the workers and give the vineyard to others. 
Mark12:10 Have you never read the Scripture passage: 'The stone that the builders rejected has become the cornerstone. 
Mark12:11 The Lord has done this, and it is amazing for us to see'?""" 
Mark12:12 They wanted to arrest him but were afraid of the crowd. They knew that he had directed this illustration at them. So they left him alone and went away. 
Mark12:13 The leaders sent some of the Pharisees and some of Herod's followers to Jesus. They wanted to trap him into saying the wrong thing. 
Mark12:14 When they came to him, they said, ""Teacher, we know that you tell the truth. You don't favor individuals because of who they are. Rather, you teach the way of God truthfully. Is it right to pay taxes to the emperor or not? Should we pay taxes or not?""" 
Mark12:15 Jesus recognized their hypocrisy, so he asked them, ""Why do you test me? Bring me a coin so that I can look at it.""" 
Mark12:16 They brought a coin. He said to them, ""Whose face and name is this?"" They told him, ""The emperor's.""" 
Mark12:17 Jesus said to them, ""Give the emperor what belongs to the emperor, and give God what belongs to God."" They were surprised at his reply." 
Mark12:18 Some Sadducees, who say that people will never come back to life, came to Jesus. They asked him," 
Mark12:19 Teacher, Moses wrote for us, 'If a man dies and leaves a wife but no child, his brother should marry his widow and have children for his brother.'" 
Mark12:20 There were seven brothers. The first got married and died without having children. 
Mark12:21 The second married her and died without having children. So did the third. 
Mark12:22 None of the seven brothers had any children. Last of all, the woman died." 
Mark12:23 When the dead come back to life, whose wife will she be? The seven brothers had married her.""" 
Mark12:24 Jesus said to them, ""Aren't you mistaken because you don't know the Scriptures or God's power?" 
Mark12:25 When the dead come back to life, they don't marry. Rather, they are like the angels in heaven." 
Mark12:26 Haven't you read in the book of Moses that the dead come back to life? It's in the passage about the bush, where God said, 'I am the God of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.'" 
Mark12:27 He's not the God of the dead but of the living. You're badly mistaken! 
Mark12:28 One of the scribes went to Jesus during the argument with the Sadducees. He saw how well Jesus answered them, so he asked him, ""Which commandment is the most important of them all?""" 
Mark12:29 Jesus answered, ""The most important is, 'Listen, Israel, the Lord our God is the only Lord." 
Mark12:30 So love the Lord your God with all your heart, with all your soul, with all your mind, and with all your strength.'" 
Mark12:31 The second most important commandment is this: 'Love your neighbor as you love yourself.' No other commandment is greater than these. 
Mark12:32 The scribe said to Jesus, ""Teacher, that was well said! You've told the truth that there is only one God and no other besides him!" 
Mark12:33 To love him with all your heart, with all your understanding, with all your strength, and to love your neighbor as you love yourself is more important than all the burnt offerings and sacrifices.""" 
Mark12:34 When Jesus heard how wisely the man answered, he told the man, ""You're not too far from the kingdom of God."" After that, no one dared to ask him another question." 
Mark12:35 While Jesus was teaching in the temple courtyard, he asked, ""How can the scribes say that the Messiah is David's son?" 
Mark12:36 David, guided by the Holy Spirit, said, 'The Lord said to my Lord: ""Take the highest position in heaven until I put your enemies under your control."" '" 
Mark12:37 David calls him Lord. So how can he be his son? The large crowd enjoyed listening to him. 
Mark12:38 As he taught, he said, ""Watch out for the scribes! They like to walk around in long robes, to be greeted in the marketplaces," 
Mark12:39 and to have the front seats in synagogues and the places of honor at dinners. 
Mark12:40 They rob widows by taking their houses and then say long prayers to make themselves look good. The scribes will receive the most severe punishment. 
Mark12:41 As Jesus sat facing the temple offering box, he watched how {much} money people put into it. Many rich people put in large amounts." 
Mark12:42 A poor widow dropped in two small coins, worth less than a cent." 
Mark12:43 He called his disciples and said to them, ""I can guarantee this truth: This poor widow has given more than all the others." 
Mark12:44 All of them have given what they could spare. But she, in her poverty, has given everything she had to live on.""" 
Mark13:1 As Jesus was going out of the temple courtyard, one of his disciples said to him, ""Teacher, look at these huge stones and these beautiful buildings!""" 
Mark13:2 Jesus said to him, ""Do you see these large buildings? Not one of these stones will be left on top of another. Each one will be torn down.""" 
Mark13:3 As Jesus was sitting on the Mount of Olives facing the temple buildings, Peter, James, John, and Andrew asked him privately," 
Mark13:3 As Jesus was sitting on the Mount of Olives facing the temple buildings, Peter, James, John, and Andrew asked him privately," 
Mark13:3 As Jesus was sitting on the Mount of Olives facing the temple buildings, Peter, James, John, and Andrew asked him privately," 
Mark13:4 Tell us, when will this happen? What will be the sign when all this will come to an end?""" 
Mark13:5 Jesus answered them, ""Be careful not to let anyone deceive you." 
Mark13:6 Many will come using my name. They will say, 'I am he,' and they will deceive many people." 
Mark13:7 When you hear of wars and rumors of wars, don't be alarmed! These things must happen, but they don't mean that the end has come." 
Mark13:8 Nation will fight against nation and kingdom against kingdom. There will be earthquakes and famines in various places. These are only the beginning pains {of the end}. 
Mark13:9 Be on your guard! People will hand you over to the Jewish courts and whip you in their synagogues. You will stand in front of governors and kings to testify to them because of me. 
Mark13:10 But first, the Good News must be spread to all nations." 
Mark13:11 When they take you away to hand you over to the authorities, don't worry ahead of time about what you will say. Instead, say whatever is given to you to say when the time comes. Indeed, you are not the one who will be speaking, but the Holy Spirit will." 
Mark13:12 Brother will hand over brother to death; a father will hand over his child. Children will rebel against their parents and kill them. 
Mark13:13 Everyone will hate you because you are committed to me. But the person who endures to the end will be saved. 
Mark13:14 When you see the disgusting thing that will cause destruction standing where it should not (let the reader take note), those of you in Judea should flee to the mountains." 
Mark13:14 When you see the disgusting thing that will cause destruction standing where it should not (let the reader take note), those of you in Judea should flee to the mountains." 
Mark13:15 Those who are on the roof should not come down to get anything out of their houses. 
Mark13:16 Those who are in the field should not turn back to get their coats. 
Mark13:17 How horrible it will be for the women who are pregnant or who are nursing babies in those days. 
Mark13:18 Pray that it will not be in winter. 
Mark13:19 It will be a time of misery that has not happened from the beginning of God's creation until now, and will certainly never happen again." 
Mark13:20 If the Lord does not reduce that time, no one will be saved. But those days will be reduced because of those whom God has chosen." 
Mark13:21 At that time don't believe anyone who tells you, 'Here is the Messiah!' or 'There he is!'" 
Mark13:22 False messiahs and false prophets will appear. They will work miraculous signs and do wonderful things to deceive, if possible, those whom God has chosen." 
Mark13:23 Be on your guard! I have told you everything before it happens. 
Mark13:24 Now, after the misery of those days, the sun will turn dark, the moon will not give light," 
Mark13:25 the stars will fall from the sky, and the powers of the universe will be shaken." 
Mark13:26 Then people will see the Son of Man coming in clouds with great power and glory. 
Mark13:27 He will send out his angels, and from every direction under the sky, they will gather those whom God has chosen." 
Mark13:28 Learn from the story of the fig tree. When its branch becomes tender and it sprouts leaves, you know summer is near." 
Mark13:29 In the same way, when you see these things happen, you know that he is near, at the door." 
Mark13:30 I can guarantee this truth: This generation will not disappear until all these things take place. 
Mark13:31 The earth and the heavens will disappear, but my words will never disappear." 
Mark13:32 No one knows when that day or hour will come. Even the angels in heaven and the Son don't know. Only the Father knows. 
Mark13:33 Be careful! Watch! You don't know the exact time. 
Mark13:34 It is like a man who went on a trip. As he left home, he put his servants in charge. He assigned work to each one and ordered the guard to be alert." 
Mark13:35 Therefore, be alert, because you don't know when the owner of the house will return. It could be in the evening or at midnight or at dawn or in the morning." 
Mark13:36 Make sure he doesn't come suddenly and find you asleep. 
Mark13:37 I'm telling everyone what I'm telling you: 'Be alert!' 
Mark14:1 It was two days before the Passover and the Festival of Unleavened Bread. The chief priests and the scribes were looking for some underhanded way to arrest Jesus and to kill him. 
Mark14:2 However, they said, ""We shouldn't arrest him during the festival, or else there will be a riot among the people.""" 
Mark14:3 Jesus was in Bethany at the home of Simon, a man who had suffered from a skin disease. While Jesus was sitting there, a woman went to him. She had a bottle of very expensive perfume made from pure nard. She opened the bottle and poured the perfume on his head." 
Mark14:4 Some who were there were irritated and said to one another, ""Why was the perfume wasted like this?" 
Mark14:5 This perfume could have been sold for a high price, and the money could have been given to the poor."" So they said some very unkind things to her." 
Mark14:6 Jesus said, ""Leave her alone! Why are you bothering her? She has done a beautiful thing for me." 
Mark14:7 You will always have the poor with you and can help them whenever you want. But you will not always have me with you. 
Mark14:8 She did what she could. She came to pour perfume on my body before it is placed in a tomb. 
Mark14:9 I can guarantee this truth: Wherever the Good News is spoken in the world, what she has done will also be told in memory of her.""" 
Mark14:10 Judas Iscariot, one of the twelve apostles, went to the chief priests to betray Jesus." 
Mark14:11 They were pleased to hear what Judas had to say and promised to give him money. So he kept looking for a chance to betray Jesus. 
Mark14:12 Killing the Passover lamb was customary on the first day of the Festival of Unleavened Bread. The disciples asked Jesus, ""Where do you want us to prepare the Passover meal for you?""" 
Mark14:13 He sent two of his disciples and told them, ""Go into the city. You will meet a man carrying a jug of water. Follow him." 
Mark14:14 When he goes into a house, tell the owner that the teacher asks, 'Where is my room where I can eat the Passover meal with my disciples?'" 
Mark14:15 He will take you upstairs and show you a large room. The room will be completely furnished. Get everything ready for us there. 
Mark14:16 The disciples left. They went into the city and found everything as Jesus had told them. So they prepared the Passover. 
Mark14:17 When evening came, Jesus arrived with the twelve apostles." 
Mark14:18 While they were at the table eating, Jesus said, ""I can guarantee this truth: One of you is going to betray me, one who is eating with me!""" 
Mark14:19 Feeling hurt, they asked him one by one, ""You don't mean me, do you?""" 
Mark14:20 He said to them, ""It's one of you twelve, someone dipping his hand into the bowl with me." 
Mark14:21 The Son of Man is going to die as the Scriptures say he will. But how horrible it will be for that person who betrays the Son of Man! It would have been better for that person if he had never been born. 
Mark14:22 While they were eating, Jesus took bread and blessed it. He broke the bread, gave it to them, and said, ""Take this. This is my body.""" 
Mark14:23 Then he took a cup, spoke a prayer of thanksgiving, and gave the cup to them. They all drank from it." 
Mark14:24 He said to them, ""This is my blood, the blood of the promise. It is poured out for many people." 
Mark14:25 I can guarantee this truth: I won't drink this wine again until that day when I drink new wine in the kingdom of God. 
Mark14:26 After they sang a hymn, they went to the Mount of Olives." 
Mark14:27 Then Jesus said to them, ""All of you will abandon me. Scripture says, 'I will strike the shepherd, and the sheep will be scattered.'" 
Mark14:28 But after I am brought back to life, I will go to Galilee ahead of you.""" 
Mark14:29 Peter said to him, ""Even if everyone else abandons you, I won't.""" 
Mark14:30 Jesus said to Peter, ""I can guarantee this truth: Tonight, before a rooster crows twice, you will say three times that you don't know me.""" 
Mark14:31 But Peter said very strongly, ""Even if I have to die with you, I will never say that I don't know you."" All the other disciples said the same thing." 
Mark14:32 Then they came to a place called Gethsemane. He said to his disciples, ""Stay here while I pray.""" 
Mark14:33 He took Peter, James, and John with him and began to feel distressed and anguished." 
Mark14:33 He took Peter, James, and John with him and began to feel distressed and anguished." 
Mark14:33 He took Peter, James, and John with him and began to feel distressed and anguished." 
Mark14:34 He said to them, ""My anguish is so great that I feel as if I'm dying. Wait here, and stay awake.""" 
Mark14:35 After walking a little farther, he fell to the ground and prayed that if it were possible he might not have to suffer what was ahead of him." 
Mark14:36 He said, ""Abba! Father! You can do anything. Take this cup {of suffering} away from me. But let your will be done rather than mine.""" 
Mark14:37 He went back and found them asleep. He said to Peter, ""Simon, are you sleeping? Couldn't you stay awake for one hour?" 
Mark14:38 Stay awake, and pray that you won't be tempted. You want to do what's right, but you're weak.""" 
Mark14:39 He went away again and prayed the same prayer as before. 
Mark14:40 He found them asleep because they couldn't keep their eyes open. They didn't even know what they should say to him. 
Mark14:41 He came back a third time and said to them, ""You might as well sleep now. It's all over. The time has come for the Son of Man to be handed over to sinners." 
Mark14:42 Get up! Let's go! The one who is betraying me is near. 
Mark14:43 Just then, while Jesus was still speaking, Judas, one of the twelve apostles, arrived. A crowd carrying swords and clubs was with him. They were from the chief priests, scribes, and leaders of the people." 
Mark14:44 Now, the traitor had given them a signal. He said, ""The one I kiss is the man you want. Arrest him, and guard him closely as you take him away.""" 
Mark14:45 Then Judas quickly stepped up to Jesus and said, ""Rabbi!"" and kissed him." 
Mark14:46 Some men took hold of Jesus and arrested him. 
Mark14:47 One of those standing there pulled out his sword and cut off the ear of the chief priest's servant. 
Mark14:48 Jesus asked them, ""Have you come out with swords and clubs to arrest me as if I were a criminal?" 
Mark14:49 I used to teach in the temple courtyard every day. But you didn't arrest me then. But what the Scriptures say must come true. 
Mark14:50 Then all the disciples abandoned him and ran away. 
Mark14:51 A certain young man was following Jesus. He had nothing on but a linen sheet. They tried to arrest him," 
Mark14:52 but he left the linen sheet behind and ran away naked. 
Mark14:53 The men took Jesus to the chief priest. All the chief priests, leaders, and scribes had gathered together." 
Mark14:54 Peter followed him at a distance and went into the chief priest's courtyard. He sat with the guards and warmed himself facing the glow of a fire. 
Mark14:55 The chief priests and the whole Jewish council were searching for some testimony against Jesus in order to execute him. But they couldn't find any. 
Mark14:56 Many gave false testimony against him, but their statements did not agree." 
Mark14:57 Then some men stood up and gave false testimony against him. They said," 
Mark14:58 We heard him say, 'I'll tear down this temple made by humans, and in three days I'll build another temple, one not made by human hands.' """ 
Mark14:59 But their testimony did not agree even on this point. 
Mark14:60 So the chief priest stood up in the center and asked Jesus, ""Don't you have any answer to what these men testify against you?""" 
Mark14:61 But he was silent. The chief priest asked him again, ""Are you the Messiah, the Son of the Blessed One?""" 
Mark14:62 Jesus answered, ""Yes, I am, and you will see the Son of Man in the highest position in heaven. He will be coming with the clouds of heaven.""" 
Mark14:63 The chief priest tore his clothes in horror and said, ""Why do we need any more witnesses?" 
Mark14:64 You've heard him dishonor God! What's your verdict? All of them condemned him with the death sentence. 
Mark14:65 Some of them began to spit on him. They covered his face and hit him with their fists. They said to him, ""Prophesy!"" Even the guards took him and slapped him." 
Mark14:66 Peter was in the courtyard. One of the chief priest's female servants 
Mark14:67 saw Peter warming himself. She looked at him and said, ""You, too, were with Jesus from Nazareth!""" 
Mark14:68 But Peter denied it by saying, ""I don't know him, and I don't understand what you're talking about."" He went to the entrance. Then a rooster crowed." 
Mark14:69 The servant saw him. Once again she said to those who were standing around, ""This man is one of them!""" 
Mark14:70 Peter again denied it. After a little while the men standing there said to Peter again, ""It's obvious you're one of them. You're a Galilean!""" 
Mark14:71 Then Peter began to curse and swear with an oath, ""I don't know this man you're talking about!""" 
Mark14:72 Just then a rooster crowed a second time. Peter remembered that Jesus said to him, ""Before a rooster crows twice, you will say three times that you don't know me."" Then Peter began to cry very hard." 
Mark15:1 Early in the morning the chief priests immediately came to a decision with the leaders and the scribes. The whole Jewish council decided to tie Jesus up, lead him away, and hand him over to Pilate." 
Mark15:2 Pilate asked him, ""Are you the king of the Jews?"" ""Yes, I am,"" Jesus answered him." 
Mark15:3 The chief priests were accusing him of many things. 
Mark15:4 So Pilate asked him again, ""Don't you have any answer? Look how many accusations they're bringing against you!""" 
Mark15:5 But Jesus no longer answered anything, so Pilate was surprised." 
Mark15:6 At every Passover festival, Pilate would free one prisoner whom the people asked for." 
Mark15:7 There was a man named Barabbas in prison. He was with some rebels who had committed murder during a riot. 
Mark15:8 The crowd asked Pilate to do for them what he always did. 
Mark15:9 Pilate answered them, ""Do you want me to free the king of the Jews for you?""" 
Mark15:10 Pilate knew that the chief priests had handed Jesus over to him because they were jealous. 
Mark15:11 The chief priests stirred up the crowd so that Pilate would free Barabbas for them instead. 
Mark15:12 So Pilate again asked them, ""Then what should I do with the king of the Jews?""" 
Mark15:13 Crucify him! they shouted back. 
Mark15:14 Pilate said to them, ""Why? What has he done wrong?"" But they shouted even louder, ""Crucify him!""" 
Mark15:15 Pilate wanted to satisfy the people, so he freed Barabbas for them. But he had Jesus whipped and handed over to be crucified." 
Mark15:16 The soldiers led Jesus into the courtyard of the palace and called together the whole troop. 
Mark15:17 They dressed him in purple, twisted some thorns into a crown, and placed it on his head." 
Mark15:18 Then they began to greet him, ""Long live the king of the Jews!""" 
Mark15:19 They kept hitting him on the head with a stick, spitting on him, and kneeling in front of him with false humility." 
Mark15:20 After the soldiers finished making fun of Jesus, they took off the purple cape and put his own clothes back on him. Then they led him out to crucify him." 
Mark15:21 A man named Simon from the city of Cyrene was coming {into Jerusalem} from his home in the country. He was the father of Alexander and Rufus. As he was about to pass by, the soldiers forced him to carry Jesus' cross." 
Mark15:22 They took Jesus to Golgotha (which means the place of the skull). 
Mark15:23 They tried to give him wine mixed with a drug called myrrh, but he wouldn't take it." 
Mark15:24 Next they crucified him. Then they divided his clothes among themselves by throwing dice to see what each one would get. 
Mark15:25 It was nine in the morning when they crucified him. 
Mark15:26 There was a written notice of the accusation against him. It read, ""The king of the Jews.""" 
Mark15:27 They crucified two criminals with him, one on his right and the other on his left." 
Mark15:28 *** 
Mark15:29 Those who passed by insulted him. They shook their heads and said, ""What a joke! You were going to tear down God's temple and build it again in three days." 
Mark15:30 Come down from the cross, and save yourself!""" 
Mark15:31 The chief priests and the scribes made fun of him among themselves in the same way. They said, ""He saved others, but he can't save himself." 
Mark15:32 Let the Messiah, the king of Israel, come down from the cross now so that we may see and believe."" Even those who were crucified with him were insulting him." 
Mark15:33 At noon darkness came over the whole land until three in the afternoon. 
Mark15:34 At three o'clock Jesus cried out in a loud voice, ""Eloi, Eloi, lema sabachthani?"" which means, ""My God, my God, why have you abandoned me?""" 
Mark15:35 When some of the people standing there heard him say that, they said, ""Listen! He's calling Elijah.""" 
Mark15:36 Someone ran and soaked a sponge in vinegar. Then he put it on a stick and offered Jesus a drink. The man said, ""Let's see if Elijah comes to take him down.""" 
Mark15:37 Then Jesus cried out in a loud voice and died. 
Mark15:38 The curtain in the temple was split in two from top to bottom. 
Mark15:39 When the officer who stood facing Jesus saw how he gave up his spirit, he said, ""Certainly, this man was the Son of God!""" 
Mark15:40 Some women were watching from a distance. Among them were Mary from Magdala, Mary (the mother of young James and Joseph), and Salome." 
Mark15:40 Some women were watching from a distance. Among them were Mary from Magdala, Mary (the mother of young James and Joseph), and Salome." 
Mark15:41 They had followed him and supported him while he was in Galilee. Many other women who had come to Jerusalem with him were there too. 
Mark15:42 It was Friday evening, before the day of worship," 
Mark15:43 when Joseph arrived. He was from the city of Arimathea and was an important member of the Jewish council. He, too, was waiting for the kingdom of God. Joseph boldly went to Pilate's quarters to ask for the body of Jesus." 
Mark15:44 Pilate wondered if Jesus had already died. So he summoned the officer to ask him if Jesus was, in fact, dead." 
Mark15:45 When the officer had assured him that Jesus was dead, Pilate let Joseph have the corpse." 
Mark15:46 Joseph had purchased some linen cloth. He took the body down from the cross and wrapped it in the cloth. Then he laid the body in a tomb, which had been cut out of rock, and he rolled a stone against the door of the tomb." 
Mark15:47 Mary from Magdala and Mary (the mother of Joses) watched where Jesus was laid. 
Mark16:1 When the day of worship was over, Mary from Magdala, Mary (the mother of James), and Salome bought spices to go and anoint Jesus." 
Mark16:1 When the day of worship was over, Mary from Magdala, Mary (the mother of James), and Salome bought spices to go and anoint Jesus." 
Mark16:2 On Sunday they were going to the tomb very early when the sun had just come up. 
Mark16:3 They said to one another, ""Who will roll away the stone for us from the entrance to the tomb?""" 
Mark16:4 When they looked up, they saw that the stone had been rolled away. It was a very large stone." 
Mark16:5 As they went into the tomb, they saw a young man. He was dressed in a white robe and sat on the right side. They were panic-stricken." 
Mark16:6 The young man said to them, ""Don't panic! You're looking for Jesus from Nazareth, who was crucified. He has been brought back to life. He's not here. Look at the place where they laid him." 
Mark16:7 Go and tell his disciples and Peter that he's going ahead of them to Galilee. There they will see him, just as he told them.""" 
Mark16:8 They went out of the tomb and ran away. Shock and trembling had overwhelmed them. They didn't say a thing to anyone, because they were afraid." 
Mark16:9 After Jesus came back to life early on Sunday, he appeared first to Mary from Magdala, from whom he had forced out seven demons." 
Mark16:10 She went and told his friends, who were grieving and crying." 
Mark16:11 They didn't believe her when they heard that he was alive and that she had seen him. 
Mark16:12 Later Jesus appeared to two disciples as they were walking to their home in the country. He did not look as he usually did. 
Mark16:13 They went back and told the others, who did not believe them either." 
Mark16:14 Still later Jesus appeared to the eleven apostles while they were eating. He put them to shame for their unbelief and because they were too stubborn to believe those who had seen him alive. 
Mark16:15 Then Jesus said to them, ""So wherever you go in the world, tell everyone the Good News." 
Mark16:16 Whoever believes and is baptized will be saved, but whoever does not believe will be condemned." 
Mark16:17 These are the miraculous signs that will accompany believers: They will use the power and authority of my name to force demons out of people. They will speak new languages. 
Mark16:18 They will pick up snakes, and if they drink any deadly poison, it will not hurt them. They will place their hands on the sick and cure them.""" 
Mark16:19 After talking with the apostles, the Lord was taken to heaven, where God gave him the highest position." 
Mark16:20 The disciples spread {the Good News} everywhere. The Lord worked with them. He confirmed his word by the miraculous signs that accompanied it. 
Luke1:1 Many have attempted to write about what had taken place among us. 
Luke1:2 They received their information from those who had been eyewitnesses and servants of God's word from the beginning, and they passed it on to us." 
Luke1:3 I, too, have followed everything closely from the beginning. So I thought it would be a good idea to write an orderly account for Your Excellency, Theophilus." 
Luke1:4 In this way you will know that what you've been told is true. 
Luke1:5 When Herod was king of Judea, there was a priest named Zechariah, who belonged to the division of priests named after Abijah. Zechariah's wife Elizabeth was a descendant of Aaron." 
Luke1:5 When Herod was king of Judea, there was a priest named Zechariah, who belonged to the division of priests named after Abijah. Zechariah's wife Elizabeth was a descendant of Aaron." 
Luke1:6 Zechariah and Elizabeth had God's approval. They followed all the Lord's commands and regulations perfectly. 
Luke1:7 Yet, they never had any children because Elizabeth couldn't become pregnant. Both of them were too old to have children." 
Luke1:8 Zechariah was on duty with his division of priests. As he served in God's presence," 
Luke1:9 he was chosen by priestly custom to go into the Lord's temple to burn incense. 
Luke1:10 All the people were praying outside while he was burning incense. 
Luke1:11 Then, to the right of the incense altar, an angel of the Lord appeared to him." 
Luke1:12 Zechariah was troubled and overcome with fear. 
Luke1:13 The angel said to him, ""Don't be afraid, Zechariah! God has heard your prayer. Your wife Elizabeth will have a son, and you will name him John." 
Luke1:13 The angel said to him, ""Don't be afraid, Zechariah! God has heard your prayer. Your wife Elizabeth will have a son, and you will name him John." 
Luke1:14 He will be your pride and joy, and many people will be glad that he was born." 
Luke1:15 As far as the Lord is concerned, he will be a great man. He will never drink wine or any other liquor. He will be filled with the Holy Spirit even before he is born." 
Luke1:16 He will bring many people in Israel back to the Lord their God. 
Luke1:17 He will go ahead of the Lord with the spirit and power that Elijah had. He will change parents' attitudes toward their children. He will change disobedient people so that they will accept the wisdom of those who have God's approval. In this way he will prepare the people for their Lord. 
Luke1:18 Zechariah said to the angel, ""What proof is there for this? I'm an old man, and my wife is beyond her childbearing years.""" 
Luke1:19 The angel answered him, ""I'm Gabriel! I stand in God's presence. God sent me to tell you this good news." 
Luke1:20 But because you didn't believe what I said, you will be unable to talk until the day this happens. Everything will come true at the right time.""" 
Luke1:21 Meanwhile, the people were waiting for Zechariah. They were amazed that he was staying in the temple so long." 
Luke1:22 When he did come out, he was unable to speak to them. So they realized that he had seen a vision in the temple. He motioned to them but remained unable to talk." 
Luke1:23 When the days of his service were over, he went home." 
Luke1:24 Later, his wife Elizabeth became pregnant and didn't go out in public for five months. She said," 
Luke1:25 The Lord has done this for me now. He has removed my public disgrace. 
Luke1:26 Six months after Elizabeth had become pregnant, God sent the angel Gabriel to Nazareth, a city in Galilee." 
Luke1:27 The angel went to a virgin promised in marriage to a descendant of David named Joseph. The virgin's name was Mary. 
Luke1:28 When the angel entered her home, he greeted her and said, ""You are favored by the Lord! The Lord is with you.""" 
Luke1:29 She was startled by what the angel said and tried to figure out what this greeting meant. 
Luke1:30 The angel told her, ""Don't be afraid, Mary. You have found favor with God." 
Luke1:31 You will become pregnant, give birth to a son, and name him Jesus." 
Luke1:32 He will be a great man and will be called the Son of the Most High. The Lord God will give him the throne of his ancestor David. 
Luke1:33 Your son will be king of Jacob's people forever, and his kingdom will never end.""" 
Luke1:34 Mary asked the angel, ""How can this be? I've never had sexual intercourse.""" 
Luke1:35 The angel answered her, ""The Holy Spirit will come to you, and the power of the Most High will overshadow you. Therefore, the holy child developing inside you will be called the Son of God." 
Luke1:36 Elizabeth, your relative, is six months pregnant with a son in her old age. People said she couldn't have a child." 
Luke1:37 But nothing is impossible for God. 
Luke1:38 Mary answered, ""I am the Lord's servant. Let everything you've said happen to me."" Then the angel left her." 
Luke1:39 Soon afterward, Mary hurried to a city in the mountain region of Judah." 
Luke1:40 She entered Zechariah's home and greeted Elizabeth. 
Luke1:41 When Elizabeth heard the greeting, she felt the baby kick. Elizabeth was filled with the Holy Spirit." 
Luke1:42 She said in a loud voice, ""You are the most blessed of all women, and blessed is the child that you will have." 
Luke1:43 I feel blessed that the mother of my Lord is visiting me. 
Luke1:44 As soon as I heard your greeting, I felt the baby jump for joy." 
Luke1:45 You are blessed for believing that the Lord would keep his promise to you. 
Luke1:46 Mary said, ""My soul praises the Lord's greatness!" 
Luke1:47 My spirit finds its joy in God, my Savior," 
Luke1:48 because he has looked favorably on me, his humble servant. ""From now on, all people will call me blessed" 
Luke1:49 because the Almighty has done great things to me. His name is holy. 
Luke1:50 For those who fear him, his mercy lasts throughout every generation." 
Luke1:51 He displayed his mighty power. He scattered those who think too highly of themselves. 
Luke1:52 He pulled strong rulers from their thrones. He honored humble people. 
Luke1:53 He fed hungry people with good food. He sent rich people away with nothing. 
Luke1:54 He remembered to help his servant Israel forever. 
Luke1:55 This is the promise he made to our ancestors, to Abraham and his descendants.""" 
Luke1:56 Mary stayed with Elizabeth about three months and then went back home. 
Luke1:57 When the time came for Elizabeth to have her child, she gave birth to a son." 
Luke1:58 Her neighbors and relatives heard that the Lord had been very kind to her, and they shared her joy."

Luke1:60 But his mother spoke up, ""Absolutely not! His name will be John."""

Luke1:61 Their friends said to her, ""But you don't have any relatives with that name."""

Luke1:66 Everyone who heard about it seriously thought it over and asked, ""What does the future hold for this child?"" It was clear that the Lord was with him." 
Luke1:67 His father Zechariah was filled with the Holy Spirit and prophesied," 
Luke1:68 Praise the Lord God of Israel! He has come to take care of his people and to set them free. 
Luke1:69 He has raised up a mighty Savior for us in the family of his servant David. 
Luke1:70 He made this promise through his holy prophets long ago. 
Luke1:71 He promised to save us from our enemies and from the power of all who hate us.

Luke2:1 At that time the Emperor Augustus ordered a census of the Roman Empire. 
Luke2:2 This was the first census taken while Quirinius was governor of Syria. 
Luke2:3 All the people went to register in the cities where their ancestors had lived. 
Luke2:4 So Joseph went from Nazareth, a city in Galilee, to a Judean city called Bethlehem. Joseph, a descendant of King David, went to Bethlehem because David had been born there." 
Luke2:4 So Joseph went from Nazareth, a city in Galilee, to a Judean city called Bethlehem. Joseph, a descendant of King David, went to Bethlehem because David had been born there." 
Luke2:5 Joseph went there to register with Mary. She had been promised to him in marriage and was pregnant. 
Luke2:6 While they were in Bethlehem, the time came for Mary to have her child." 
Luke2:7 She gave birth to her firstborn son. She wrapped him in strips of cloth and laid him in a manger because there wasn't any room for them in the inn. 
Luke2:8 Shepherds were in the fields near Bethlehem. They were taking turns watching their flock during the night. 
Luke2:9 An angel from the Lord suddenly appeared to them. The glory of the Lord filled the area with light, and they were terrified." 
Luke2:10 The angel said to them, ""Don't be afraid! I have good news for you, a message that will fill everyone with joy." 
Luke2:11 Today your Savior, Christ the Lord, was born in David's city." 
Luke2:12 This is how you will recognize him: You will find an infant wrapped in strips of cloth and lying in a manger. 
Luke2:13 Suddenly, a large army of angels appeared with the angel. They were praising God by saying," 
Luke2:14 Glory to God in the highest heaven, and on earth peace to those who have his good will!""" 
Luke2:15 The angels left them and went back to heaven. The shepherds said to each other, ""Let's go to Bethlehem and see what the Lord has told us about.""" 
Luke2:16 They went quickly and found Mary and Joseph with the baby, who was lying in a manger." 
Luke2:17 When they saw the child, they repeated what they had been told about him." 
Luke2:18 Everyone who heard the shepherds' story was amazed. 
Luke2:19 Mary treasured all these things in her heart and always thought about them. 
Luke2:20 As the shepherds returned to their flock, they glorified and praised God for everything they had seen and heard. Everything happened the way the angel had told them." 
Luke2:21 Eight days after his birth, the child was circumcised and named Jesus. This was the name the angel had given him before his mother became pregnant." 
Luke2:22 After the days required by Moses' Teachings to make a mother clean had passed, Joseph and Mary went to Jerusalem. They took Jesus to present him to the Lord." 
Luke2:23 They did exactly what was written in the Lord's Teachings: Every firstborn boy is to be set apart as holy to the Lord. 
Luke2:24 They also offered a sacrifice as required by the Lord's Teachings: a pair of mourning doves or two young pigeons. 
Luke2:25 A man named Simeon was in Jerusalem. He lived an honorable and devout life. He was waiting for the one who would comfort Israel. The Holy Spirit was with Simeon 
Luke2:26 and had told him that he wouldn't die until he had seen the Messiah, whom the Lord would send." 
Luke2:27 Moved by the Spirit, Simeon went into the temple courtyard. Mary and Joseph were bringing the child Jesus into the courtyard at the same time. They brought him so that they could do for him what Moses' Teachings required." 
Luke2:28 Then Simeon took the child in his arms and praised God by saying," 
Luke2:29 Now, Lord, you are allowing your servant to leave in peace as you promised." 
Luke2:30 My eyes have seen your salvation," 
Luke2:31 which you have prepared for all people to see. 
Luke2:32 He is a light that will reveal {salvation} to the nations and bring glory to your people Israel. 
Luke2:33 Jesus' father and mother were amazed at what was said about him. 
Luke2:34 Then Simeon blessed them and said to Mary, his mother, ""This child is the reason that many people in Israel will be condemned and many others will be saved. He will be a sign that will expose" 
Luke2:35 the thoughts of those who reject him. And a sword will pierce your heart. 
Luke2:36 Anna, a prophet, was also there. She was a descendant of Phanuel from the tribe of Asher. She was now very old. Her husband had died seven years after they were married," 
Luke2:37 and she had been a widow for 84 years. Anna never left the temple courtyard but worshiped day and night by fasting and praying. 
Luke2:38 At that moment she came up to Mary and Joseph and began to thank God. She spoke about Jesus to all who were waiting for Jerusalem to be set free. 
Luke2:39 After doing everything the Lord's Teachings required, Joseph and Mary returned to their hometown of Nazareth in Galilee." 
Luke2:40 The child grew and became strong. He was filled with wisdom, and God's favor was with him." 
Luke2:41 Every year Jesus' parents would go to Jerusalem for the Passover festival. 
Luke2:42 When he was 12 years old, they went as usual." 
Luke2:43 When the festival was over, they left for home. The boy Jesus stayed behind in Jerusalem, but his parents didn't know it." 
Luke2:44 They thought that he was with the others who were traveling with them. After traveling for a day, they started to look for him among their relatives and friends." 
Luke2:45 When they didn't find him, they went back to Jerusalem to look for him." 
Luke2:46 Three days later, they found him in the temple courtyard. He was sitting among the teachers, listening to them, and asking them questions." 
Luke2:47 His understanding and his answers stunned everyone who heard him. 
Luke2:48 When his parents saw him, they were shocked. His mother asked him, ""Son, why have you done this to us? Your father and I have been worried sick looking for you!""" 
Luke2:49 Jesus said to them, ""Why were you looking for me? Didn't you realize that I had to be in my Father's house?""" 
Luke2:50 But they didn't understand what he meant. 
Luke2:51 Then he returned with them to Nazareth and was obedient to them. His mother treasured all these things in her heart. 
Luke2:52 Jesus grew in wisdom and maturity. He gained favor from God and people.

Luke4:1 Jesus was filled with the Holy Spirit as he left the Jordan River. The Spirit led him while he was in the desert," 
Luke4:2 where he was tempted by the devil for 40 days. During those days Jesus ate nothing, so when they were over, he was hungry." 
Luke4:3 The devil said to him, ""If you are the Son of God, tell this stone to become a loaf of bread.""" 
Luke4:4 Jesus answered him, ""Scripture says, 'A person cannot live on bread alone.' """ 
Luke4:5 The devil took him to a high place and showed him all the kingdoms of the world in an instant. 
Luke4:6 The devil said to him, ""I will give you all the power and glory of these kingdoms. All of it has been given to me, and I give it to anyone I please." 
Luke4:7 So if you will worship me, all this will be yours.""" 
Luke4:8 Jesus answered him, ""Scripture says, 'Worship the Lord your God and serve only him.' """ 
Luke4:9 Then the devil took him into Jerusalem and had him stand on the highest part of the temple. He said to Jesus, ""If you are the Son of God, jump from here!" 
Luke4:10 Scripture says, 'He will put his angels in charge of you to watch over you carefully." 
Luke4:11 They will carry you in their hands so that you never hit your foot against a rock.' 
Luke4:12 Jesus answered him, ""It has been said, 'Never tempt the Lord your God.' """ 
Luke4:13 After the devil had finished tempting Jesus in every possible way, the devil left him until another time." 
Luke4:14 Jesus returned to Galilee. The power of the Spirit was with him, and the news about him spread throughout the surrounding country." 
Luke4:15 He taught in the synagogues, and everyone praised him." 
Luke4:16 Then Jesus came to Nazareth, where he had been brought up. As usual he went into the synagogue on the day of worship. He stood up to read the lesson." 
Luke4:17 The attendant gave him the book of the prophet Isaiah. He opened it and found the place where it read: 
Luke4:18 The Spirit of the Lord is with me. He has anointed me to tell the Good News to the poor. He has sent me to announce forgiveness to the prisoners of sin and the restoring of sight to the blind, to forgive those who have been shattered by sin," 
Luke4:19 to announce the year of the Lord's favor. 
Luke4:20 Jesus closed the book, gave it back to the attendant, and sat down. Everyone in the synagogue watched him closely." 
Luke4:21 Then he said to them, ""This passage came true today when you heard me read it.""" 
Luke4:22 All the people spoke well of him. They were amazed to hear the gracious words flowing from his lips. They said, ""Isn't this Joseph's son?""" 
Luke4:23 So he said to them, ""You'll probably quote this proverb to me, 'Doctor, cure yourself!' and then say to me, 'Do all the things in your hometown that we've heard you've done in Capernaum.' """ 
Luke4:24 Then Jesus added, ""I can guarantee this truth: A prophet isn't accepted in his hometown." 
Luke4:25 I can guarantee this truth: There were many widows in Israel in Elijah's time. It had not rained for three-and-a-half years, and the famine was severe everywhere in the country." 
Luke4:26 But God didn't send Elijah to anyone except a widow at Zarephath in the territory of Sidon. 
Luke4:27 There were also many people with skin diseases in Israel in the prophet Elisha's time. But God cured no one except Naaman from Syria. 
Luke4:28 Everyone in the synagogue became furious when they heard this. 
Luke4:29 Their city was built on a hill with a cliff. So they got up, forced Jesus out of the city, and led him to the cliff. They intended to throw him off of it." 
Luke4:30 But Jesus walked right by them and went away. 
Luke4:31 Jesus went to Capernaum, a city in Galilee, and taught them on a day of worship." 
Luke4:32 The people were amazed at his teachings because he spoke with authority. 
Luke4:33 In the synagogue was a man possessed by a spirit, an evil demon. He shouted very loudly," 
Luke4:34 Oh, no! What do you want with us, Jesus from Nazareth? Have you come to destroy us? I know who you are—the Holy One of God!""" 
Luke4:35 Jesus ordered the spirit, ""Keep quiet, and come out of him!"" The demon threw the man down in the middle of the synagogue and came out without hurting him." 
Luke4:36 Everyone was stunned. They said to one another, ""What kind of command is this? With authority and power he gives orders to evil spirits, and they come out.""" 
Luke4:37 So news about him spread to every place throughout the surrounding region. 
Luke4:38 Jesus left the synagogue and went to Simon's house. Simon's mother-in-law was sick with a high fever. They asked Jesus to help her. 
Luke4:39 He bent over her, ordered the fever to leave, and it went away. She got up immediately and prepared a meal for them." 
Luke4:40 When the sun was setting, everyone who had friends suffering from various diseases brought them to him. He placed his hands on each of them and cured them." 
Luke4:41 Demons came out of many people, shouting, ""You are the Son of God!"" But Jesus ordered them not to speak. After all, they knew he was the Messiah."

Luke5:1 One day Jesus was standing by the Sea of Galilee. The people crowded around him as they listened to God's word. 
Luke5:2 Jesus saw two boats on the shore. The fishermen had stepped out of them and were washing their nets. 
Luke5:3 So Jesus got into the boat that belonged to Simon and asked him to push off a little from the shore. Then Jesus sat down and taught the crowd from the boat. 
Luke5:4 When he finished speaking, he told Simon, ""Take the boat into deep water, and lower your nets to catch some fish.""" 
Luke5:5 Simon answered, ""Teacher, we worked hard all night and caught nothing. But if you say so, I'll lower the nets.""" 
Luke5:6 After the men had done this, they caught such a large number of fish that their nets began to tear." 
Luke5:7 So they signaled to their partners in the other boat to come and help them. Their partners came and filled both boats until the boats nearly sank. 
Luke5:8 When Simon Peter saw this, he knelt in front of Jesus and said, ""Leave me, Lord! I'm a sinful person!""" 
Luke5:9 Simon and everyone who was with him was amazed to see the large number of fish they had caught. 
Luke5:10 James and John, who were Zebedee's sons and Simon's partners, were also amazed. Jesus told Simon, ""Don't be afraid. From now on you will catch people instead of fish."""

Luke5:11 Simon and his partners brought the boats to shore, left everything, and followed Jesus." 
Luke5:12 One day Jesus was in a city where there was a man covered with a serious skin disease. When the man saw Jesus, he bowed with his face to the ground. He begged Jesus, ""Sir, if you want to, you can make me clean.""" 
Luke5:13 Jesus reached out, touched him, and said, ""I want to. So be clean!"" Immediately, his skin disease went away." 
Luke5:14 Jesus ordered him, ""Don't tell anyone. Instead, show yourself to the priest. Then offer the sacrifice as Moses commanded as proof to people that you are clean.""" 
Luke5:15 The news about Jesus spread even more. Large crowds gathered to hear him and have their diseases cured. 
Luke5:16 But he would go away to places where he could be alone for prayer.

Luke5:17 One day when Jesus was teaching, some Pharisees and experts in Moses' Teachings were present. They had come from every village in Galilee and Judea and from Jerusalem. Jesus had the power of the Lord to heal." 
Luke5:18 Some men brought a paralyzed man on a stretcher. They tried to take him into the house and put him in front of Jesus. 
Luke5:19 But they could not find a way to get him into the house because of the crowd. So they went up on the roof. They made an opening in the tiles and let the man down on his stretcher among the people. (They lowered him in front of Jesus.) 
Luke5:20 When Jesus saw their faith, he said, ""Sir, your sins are forgiven.""" 
Luke5:21 The scribes and the Pharisees thought, ""Who is this man? He's dishonoring God! Who besides God can forgive sins?""" 
Luke5:22 Jesus knew what they were thinking. So he said to them, ""What are you thinking?" 
Luke5:23 Is it easier to say, 'Your sins are forgiven,' or to say, 'Get up and walk'?" 
Luke5:24 I want you to know that the Son of Man has authority on earth to forgive sins. Then he said to the paralyzed man, ""Get up, pick up your stretcher, and go home.""" 
Luke5:25 The man immediately stood up in front of them and picked up the stretcher he had been lying on. Praising God, he went home." 
Luke5:26 Everyone was amazed and praised God. They were filled with awe and said, ""We've seen things today we can hardly believe!""" 
Luke5:27 After that, Jesus left. He saw a tax collector named Levi sitting in a tax office. Jesus said to him, ""Follow me!""" 
Luke5:28 So Levi got up, left everything, and followed him." 
Luke5:29 Levi held a large reception at his home for Jesus. A huge crowd of tax collectors and others were eating with them. 
Luke5:30 The Pharisees and their scribes complained to Jesus' disciples. They asked, ""Why do you eat and drink with tax collectors and sinners?""" 
Luke5:31 Jesus answered them, ""Healthy people don't need a doctor; those who are sick do." 
Luke5:32 I've come to call sinners to change the way they think and act, not to call people who think they have God's approval.""" 
Luke5:33 They said to him, ""John's disciples frequently fast and say prayers, and so do the disciples of the Pharisees. But your disciples eat and drink.""" 
Luke5:33 They said to him, ""John's disciples frequently fast and say prayers, and so do the disciples of the Pharisees. But your disciples eat and drink.""" 
Luke5:34 Jesus asked them, ""Can you force wedding guests to fast while the groom is still with them?" 
Luke5:35 The time will come when the groom will be taken away from them. At that time they will fast. 
Luke5:36 He also used these illustrations: No one tears a piece of cloth from a new coat to patch an old coat. Otherwise, the new cloth will tear the old. Besides, the patch from the new will not match the old." 
Luke5:37 People don't pour new wine into old wineskins. If they do, the new wine will make the skins burst. The wine will run out, and the skins will be ruined." 
Luke5:38 Rather, new wine is to be poured into fresh skins." 
Luke5:39 No one who has been drinking old wine wants new wine. He says, 'The old wine is better!' """ 
Luke6:1 Once, on a day of worship, Jesus was walking through some grainfields. His disciples were picking the heads of grain, removing the husks, and eating the grain." 
Luke6:2 Some of the Pharisees asked, ""Why are your disciples doing something that is not right to do on the day of worship?""" 
Luke6:3 Jesus answered them, ""Haven't you read what David did when he and his men were hungry?" 
Luke6:4 Haven't you read how he went into the house of God, ate the bread of the presence, and gave some of it to the men who were with him? He had no right to eat those loaves. Only the priests have that right.""" 
Luke6:5 Then he added, ""The Son of Man has authority over the day of worship.""" 
Luke6:6 On another day of worship, Jesus went into a synagogue to teach. A man whose right hand was paralyzed was there." 
Luke6:7 The scribes and the Pharisees were watching Jesus closely. They wanted to see whether he would heal the man on the day of worship so that they could find a way to accuse him of doing something wrong. 
Luke6:8 But Jesus knew what they were thinking. So he told the man with the paralyzed hand, ""Get up, and stand in the center {of the synagogue}!"" The man got up and stood there." 
Luke6:9 Then Jesus said to them, ""I ask you—what is the right thing to do on a day of worship: to do good or evil, to give a person his health or to destroy it?""" 
Luke6:10 He looked around at all of them and then said to the man, ""Hold out your hand."" The man did so, and his hand became normal again." 
Luke6:11 The scribes and Pharisees were furious and began to discuss with each other what they could do to Jesus.

Luke6:18 They wanted to hear him and be cured of their diseases. Those who were tormented by evil spirits were cured. 
Luke6:19 The entire crowd was trying to touch him because power was coming from him and curing all of them. 
Luke6:20 Jesus looked at his disciples and said, ""Blessed are those who are poor. The kingdom of God is theirs." 
Luke6:21 Blessed are those who are hungry. They will be satisfied. Blessed are those who are crying. They will laugh. 
Luke6:22 Blessed are you when people hate you, avoid you, insult you, and slander you because you are committed to the Son of Man." 
Luke6:23 Rejoice then, and be very happy! You have a great reward in heaven. That's the way their ancestors treated the prophets." 
Luke6:24 But how horrible it will be for those who are rich. They have had their comfort. 
Luke6:25 How horrible it will be for those who are well-fed. They will be hungry. How horrible it will be for those who are laughing. They will mourn and cry. 
Luke6:26 How horrible it will be for you when everyone says nice things about you. That's the way their ancestors treated the false prophets. 
Luke6:27 But I tell everyone who is listening: Love your enemies. Be kind to those who hate you. 
Luke6:28 Bless those who curse you. Pray for those who insult you. 
Luke6:29 If someone strikes you on the cheek, offer the other cheek as well. If someone takes your coat, don't stop him from taking your shirt." 
Luke6:30 Give to everyone who asks you for something. If someone takes what is yours, don't insist on getting it back." 
Luke6:31 Do for other people everything you want them to do for you. 
Luke6:32 If you love those who love you, do you deserve any thanks for that? Even sinners love those who love them." 
Luke6:33 If you help those who help you, do you deserve any thanks for that? Sinners do that too." 
Luke6:34 If you lend anything to those from whom you expect to get something back, do you deserve any thanks for that? Sinners also lend to sinners to get back what they lend." 
Luke6:35 Rather, love your enemies, help them, and lend to them without expecting to get anything back. Then you will have a great reward. You will be the children of the Most High God. After all, he is kind to unthankful and evil people." 
Luke6:36 Be merciful as your Father is merciful. 
Luke6:37 Stop judging, and you will never be judged. Stop condemning, and you will never be condemned. Forgive, and you will be forgiven." 
Luke6:38 Give, and you will receive. A large quantity, pressed together, shaken down, and running over will be put into your pocket. The standards you use for others will be applied to you.""" 
Luke6:39 Jesus also gave them this illustration: Can one blind person lead another? Won't both fall into the same pit? 
Luke6:40 A student is no better than his teacher. But everyone who is well-trained will be like his teacher. 
Luke6:41 Why do you see the piece of sawdust in another believer's eye and not notice the wooden beam in your own eye? 
Luke6:42 How can you say to another believer, 'Friend, let me take the piece of sawdust out of your eye,' when you don't see the beam in your own eye? You hypocrite! First remove the beam from your own eye. Then you will see clearly to remove the piece of sawdust from another believer's eye." 
Luke6:43 A good tree doesn't produce rotten fruit, and a rotten tree doesn't produce good fruit." 
Luke6:44 Each tree is known by its fruit. You don't pick figs from thorny plants or grapes from a thornbush. 
Luke6:45 Good people do the good that is in them. But evil people do the evil that is in them. The things people say come from inside them. 
Luke6:46 Why do you call me Lord but don't do what I tell you? 
Luke6:47 I will show you what everyone who comes to me, hears what I say, and obeys it is like." 
Luke6:48 He is like a person who dug down to bedrock to lay the foundation of his home. When a flood came, the floodwaters pushed against that house. But the house couldn't be washed away because it had a good foundation." 
Luke6:49 The person who hears {what I say} but doesn't obey it is like someone who built a house on the ground without any foundation. The floodwaters pushed against it, and that house quickly collapsed and was destroyed."""

Luke7:28 I can guarantee that of all the people ever born, no one is greater than John. Yet, the least important person in the kingdom of God is greater than John." 
Luke7:28 I can guarantee that of all the people ever born, no one is greater than John. Yet, the least important person in the kingdom of God is greater than John." 
Luke7:29 All the people, including tax collectors, heard John. They admitted that God was right by letting John baptize them." 
Luke7:29 All the people, including tax collectors, heard John. They admitted that God was right by letting John baptize them." 
Luke7:30 But the Pharisees and the experts in Moses' Teachings rejected God's plan for them. They refused to be baptized. 
Luke7:31 How can I describe the people who are living now? What are they like? 
Luke7:32 They are like children who sit in the marketplace and shout to each other, 'We played music for you, but you didn't dance. We sang a funeral song, but you didn't cry.'" 
Luke7:33 John the Baptizer has come neither eating bread nor drinking wine, and you say, 'There's a demon in him!'" 
Luke7:33 John the Baptizer has come neither eating bread nor drinking wine, and you say, 'There's a demon in him!'" 
Luke7:34 The Son of Man has come eating and drinking, and you say, 'Look at him! He's a glutton and a drunk, a friend of tax collectors and sinners!'" 
Luke7:35 Yet, wisdom is proved right by all its results.""" 
Luke7:36 One of the Pharisees invited Jesus to eat with him. Jesus went to the Pharisee's house and was eating at the table. 
Luke7:37 A woman who lived a sinful life in that city found out that Jesus was eating at the Pharisee's house. So she took a bottle of perfume 
Luke7:38 and knelt at his feet. She was crying and washed his feet with her tears. Then she dried his feet with her hair, kissed them over and over again, and poured the perfume on them." 
Luke7:39 The Pharisee who had invited Jesus saw this and thought, ""If this man really were a prophet, he would know what sort of woman is touching him. She's a sinner.""" 
Luke7:40 Jesus spoke up, ""Simon, I have something to say to you."" Simon replied, ""Teacher, you're free to speak.""" 
Luke7:41 {So Jesus said,} ""Two men owed a moneylender some money. One owed him five hundred silver coins, and the other owed him fifty." 
Luke7:42 When they couldn't pay it back, he was kind enough to cancel their debts. Now, who do you think will love him the most?""" 
Luke7:43 Simon answered, ""I suppose the one who had the largest debt canceled."" Jesus said to him, ""You're right!""" 
Luke7:44 Then, turning to the woman, he said to Simon, ""You see this woman, don't you? I came into your house. You didn't wash my feet. But she has washed my feet with her tears and dried them with her hair." 
Luke7:45 You didn't give me a kiss. But ever since I came in, she has not stopped kissing my feet." 
Luke7:46 You didn't put any olive oil on my head. But she has poured perfume on my feet. 
Luke7:47 That's why I'm telling you that her many sins have been forgiven. Her great love proves that. But whoever receives little forgiveness loves very little. 
Luke7:48 Then Jesus said to her, ""Your sins have been forgiven.""" 
Luke7:49 The other guests thought, ""Who is this man who even forgives sins?""" 
Luke7:50 Jesus said to the woman, ""Your faith has saved you. Go in peace!""" 
Luke8:1 After this, Jesus traveled from one city and village to another. He spread the Good News about God's kingdom. The twelve apostles were with him." 
Luke8:2 Also, some women were with him. They had been cured from evil spirits and various illnesses. These women were Mary, also called Magdalene, from whom seven demons had gone out;" 
Luke8:3 Joanna, whose husband Chusa was Herod's administrator; Susanna; and many other women. They provided financial support for Jesus and his disciples." 
Luke8:4 When a large crowd had gathered and people had come to Jesus from every city, he used this story as an illustration:" 
Luke8:5 A farmer went to plant his seeds. Some seeds were planted along the road, were trampled, and were devoured by birds."
