Luke21:34 Make sure that you don't become drunk, hung over, and worried about life. Then that day could suddenly catch you by surprise" 
Luke21:35 like a trap that catches a bird. That day will surprise all people who live on the earth. 
Luke21:36 Be alert at all times. Pray so that you have the power to escape everything that is about to happen and to stand in front of the Son of Man. 
Luke21:37 During the day Jesus would teach in the temple courtyard. But at night he would go to the Mount of Olives (as it was called) and spend the night there. 
Luke21:38 All of the people would get up early to hear him speak in the temple courtyard. 
Luke22:1 The Festival of Unleavened Bread, called Passover, was near." 
Luke22:2 The chief priests and the scribes were looking for some way to kill Jesus. However, they were afraid of the people." 
Luke22:3 Then Satan entered Judas Iscariot, one of the twelve apostles." 
Luke22:4 Judas went to the chief priests and the temple guards and discussed with them how he could betray Jesus. 
Luke22:5 They were pleased and agreed to give him some money. 
Luke22:6 So Judas promised to do it. He kept looking for an opportunity to betray Jesus to them when there was no crowd. 
Luke22:7 The day came during the Festival of Unleavened Bread when the Passover lamb had to be killed. 
Luke22:8 Jesus sent Peter and John and told them, ""Go, prepare the Passover lamb for us to eat.""" 
Luke22:8 Jesus sent Peter and John and told them, ""Go, prepare the Passover lamb for us to eat.""" 
Luke22:9 They asked him, ""Where do you want us to prepare it?""" 
Luke22:10 He told them, ""Go into the city, and you will meet a man carrying a jug of water. Follow him into the house he enters." 
Luke22:11 Tell the owner of the house that the teacher asks, 'Where is the room where I can eat the Passover meal with my disciples?'" 
Luke22:12 He will take you upstairs and show you a large furnished room. Get things ready there. 
Luke22:13 The disciples left. They found everything as Jesus had told them and prepared the Passover. 
Luke22:14 When it was time to eat the Passover meal, Jesus and the apostles were at the table." 
Luke22:15 Jesus said to them, ""I've had a deep desire to eat this Passover with you before I suffer." 
Luke22:16 I can guarantee that I won't eat it again until it finds its fulfillment in the kingdom of God. 
Luke22:17 Then he took a cup and spoke a prayer of thanksgiving. He said, ""Take this, and share it." 
Luke22:18 I can guarantee that from now on I won't drink this wine until the kingdom of God comes. 
Luke22:19 Then Jesus took bread and spoke a prayer of thanksgiving. He broke the bread, gave it to them, and said, ""This is my body, which is given up for you. Do this to remember me.""" 
Luke22:20 When supper was over, he did the same with the cup. He said, ""This cup that is poured out for you is the new promise made with my blood.""" 
Luke22:21 The hand of the one who will betray me is with me on the table. 
Luke22:22 The Son of Man is going to die the way it has been planned for him. But how horrible it will be for that person who betrays him. 
Luke22:23 So they began to discuss with each other who could do such a thing. 
Luke22:24 Then a quarrel broke out among the disciples. They argued about who should be considered the greatest. 
Luke22:25 Jesus said to them, ""The kings of nations have power over their people, and those in authority call themselves friends of the people." 
Luke22:26 But you're not going to be that way! Rather, the greatest among you must be like the youngest, and your leader must be like a servant." 
Luke22:27 Who's the greatest, the person who sits at the table or the servant? Isn't it really the person who sits at the table? But I'm among you as a servant." 
Luke22:28 You have stood by me in the troubles that have tested me. 
Luke22:29 So as my Father has given me a kingdom, I'm giving it to you." 
Luke22:30 You will eat and drink at my table in my kingdom. You will also sit on thrones and judge the twelve tribes of Israel. 
Luke22:31 {Then the Lord said,} ""Simon, Simon, listen! Satan has demanded to have you apostles for himself. He wants to separate you from me as a farmer separates wheat from husks." 
Luke22:32 But I have prayed for you, Simon, that your faith will not fail. So when you recover, strengthen the other disciples.""" 
Luke22:33 But Peter said to him, ""Lord, I'm ready to go to prison with you and to die with you.""" 
Luke22:34 Jesus replied, ""Peter, I can guarantee that the rooster won't crow tonight until you say three times that you don't know me.""" 
Luke22:35 Then Jesus said to them, ""When I sent you out without a wallet, traveling bag, or sandals, you didn't lack anything, did you?"" ""Not a thing!"" they answered." 
Luke22:36 Then he said to them, ""But now, the person who has a wallet and a traveling bag should take them along. The person who doesn't have a sword should sell his coat and buy one." 
Luke22:37 I can guarantee that the Scripture passage which says, 'He was counted with criminals,' must find its fulfillment in me. Indeed, whatever is written about me will come true.""" 
Luke22:38 The disciples said, ""Lord, look! Here are two swords!"" Then Jesus said to them, ""That's enough!""" 
Luke22:39 Jesus went out {of the city} to the Mount of Olives as he usually did. His disciples followed him. 
Luke22:40 When he arrived, he said to them, ""Pray that you won't be tempted.""" 
Luke22:41 Then he withdrew from them about a stone's throw, knelt down, and prayed," 
Luke22:42 Father, if it is your will, take this cup {of suffering} away from me. However, your will must be done, not mine.""" 
Luke22:43 Then an angel from heaven appeared to him and gave him strength. 
Luke22:44 So he prayed very hard in anguish. His sweat became like drops of blood falling to the ground. 
Luke22:45 When Jesus ended his prayer, he got up and went to the disciples. He found them asleep and overcome with sadness." 
Luke22:46 He said to them, ""Why are you sleeping? Get up, and pray that you won't be tempted.""" 
Luke22:47 While he was still speaking to the disciples, a crowd arrived. The man called Judas, one of the twelve apostles, was leading them. He came close to Jesus to kiss him." 
Luke22:48 Jesus said to him, ""Judas, do you intend to betray the Son of Man with a kiss?""" 
Luke22:49 The men who were with Jesus saw what was going to happen. So they asked him, ""Lord, should we use our swords to fight?""" 
Luke22:50 One of the disciples cut off the right ear of the chief priest's servant. 
Luke22:51 But Jesus said, ""Stop! That's enough of this."" Then he touched the servant's ear and healed him." 
Luke22:52 Then Jesus said to the chief priests, temple guards, and leaders who had come for him, ""Have you come out with swords and clubs as if I were a criminal?" 
Luke22:53 I was with you in the temple courtyard every day and you didn't try to arrest me. But this is your time, when darkness rules.""" 
Luke22:54 So they arrested Jesus and led him away to the chief priest's house. Peter followed at a distance. 
Luke22:55 Some men had lit a fire in the middle of the courtyard. As they sat together, Peter sat among them." 
Luke22:56 A female servant saw him as he sat facing the glow of the fire. She stared at him and said, ""This man was with Jesus.""" 
Luke22:57 But Peter denied it by saying, ""I don't know him, woman.""" 
Luke22:58 A little later someone else saw Peter and said, ""You are one of them."" But Peter said, ""Not me!""" 
Luke22:59 About an hour later another person insisted, ""It's obvious that this man was with him. He's a Galilean!""" 
Luke22:60 But Peter said, ""I don't know what you're talking about!"" Just then, while he was still speaking, a rooster crowed." 
Luke22:61 Then the Lord turned and looked directly at Peter. Peter remembered what the Lord had said: Before a rooster crows today, you will say three times that you don't know me.""" 
Luke22:62 Then Peter went outside and cried bitterly. 
Luke22:63 The men who were guarding Jesus made fun of him as they beat him. 
Luke22:64 They blindfolded him and said to him, ""Tell us who hit you.""" 
Luke22:65 They also insulted him in many other ways. 
Luke22:66 In the morning the council of the people's leaders, the chief priests and the scribes, gathered together. They brought Jesus in front of their highest court and asked him," 
Luke22:67 Tell us, are you the Messiah?"" Jesus said to them, ""If I tell you, you won't believe me." 
Luke22:68 And if I ask you, you won't answer." 
Luke22:69 But from now on, the Son of Man will be in the highest position in heaven.""" 
Luke22:70 Then all of them said, ""So you're the Son of God?"" Jesus answered them, ""You're right to say that I am.""" 
Luke22:71 Then they said, ""Why do we need any more testimony? We've heard him say it ourselves.""" 
Luke23:1 Then the entire assembly stood up and took him to Pilate. 
Luke23:2 They began to accuse Jesus by saying, ""We found that he stirs up trouble among our people: He keeps them from paying taxes to the emperor, and he says that he is Christ, a king.""" 
Luke23:3 Pilate asked him, ""Are you the king of the Jews?"" ""Yes, I am,"" Jesus answered." 
Luke23:4 Pilate said to the chief priests and the crowd, ""I can't find this man guilty of any crime.""" 
Luke23:5 The priests and the crowd became more forceful. They said, ""He stirs up the people throughout Judea with his teachings. He started in Galilee and has come here.""" 
Luke23:5 The priests and the crowd became more forceful. They said, ""He stirs up the people throughout Judea with his teachings. He started in Galilee and has come here.""" 
Luke23:6 When Pilate heard that, he asked if the man was from Galilee." 
Luke23:7 When Pilate found out that he was, he sent Jesus to Herod. Herod ruled Galilee and was in Jerusalem at that time." 
Luke23:8 Herod was very pleased to see Jesus. For a long time he had wanted to see him. He had heard about Jesus and hoped to see him perform some kind of miracle. 
Luke23:9 Herod asked Jesus many questions, but Jesus wouldn't answer him." 
Luke23:10 Meanwhile, the chief priests and the scribes stood there and shouted their accusations against Jesus." 
Luke23:11 Herod and his soldiers treated Jesus with contempt and made fun of him. They put a colorful robe on him and sent him back to Pilate. 
Luke23:12 So Herod and Pilate became friends that day. They had been enemies before this. 
Luke23:13 Then Pilate called together the chief priests, the rulers, and the people." 
Luke23:14 He told them, ""You brought me this man as someone who turns the people against the government. I've questioned him in front of you and haven't found this man guilty of the crimes of which you accuse him." 
Luke23:15 Neither could Herod. So he sent this man back to us. This man hasn't done anything to deserve the death penalty. 
Luke23:16 So I'm going to have him whipped and set free. 
Luke23:17 *** 
Luke23:18 The whole crowd then shouted, ""Take him away! Free Barabbas for us.""" 
Luke23:19 (Barabbas had been thrown into prison for his involvement in a riot that had taken place in the city and for murder.) 
Luke23:20 But because Pilate wanted to free Jesus, he spoke to the people again." 
Luke23:21 They began yelling, ""Crucify him! Crucify him!""" 
Luke23:22 A third time Pilate spoke to them. He asked, ""Why? What has he done wrong? I haven't found this man deserving of the death penalty. So I'm going to have him whipped and set free.""" 
Luke23:23 But the crowd pressured Pilate. They shouted that Jesus had to be crucified, and they finally won." 
Luke23:24 Pilate decided to give in to their demand. 
Luke23:25 He freed Barabbas, who had been put in prison for rioting and murdering, because that's what they wanted. But he let them do what they wanted to Jesus." 
Luke23:26 As the soldiers led Jesus away, they grabbed a man named Simon, who was from the city of Cyrene. Simon was coming into Jerusalem. They laid the cross on him and made him carry it behind Jesus." 
Luke23:27 A large crowd followed Jesus. The women in the crowd cried and sang funeral songs for him. 
Luke23:28 Jesus turned to them and said, ""You women of Jerusalem, don't cry for me! Rather, cry for yourselves and your children!" 
Luke23:29 The time is coming when people will say, 'Blessed are the women who couldn't get pregnant, who couldn't give birth, and who couldn't nurse a child.'" 
Luke23:30 Then people will say to the mountains, 'Fall on us!' and to the hills, 'Cover us!'" 
Luke23:31 If people do this to a green tree, what will happen to a dry one?""" 
Luke23:32 Two others, who were criminals, were led away to be executed with him." 
Luke23:33 When they came to the place called The Skull, they crucified him. The criminals were also crucified, one on his right and the other on his left." 
Luke23:34 Then Jesus said, ""Father, forgive them. They don't know what they're doing."" Meanwhile, the soldiers divided his clothes among themselves by throwing dice." 
Luke23:35 The people stood there watching. But the rulers were making sarcastic remarks. They said, ""He saved others. If he's the Messiah that God has chosen, let him save himself!""" 
Luke23:36 The soldiers also made fun of him. They would go up to him, offer him some vinegar," 
Luke23:37 and say, ""If you're the king of the Jews, save yourself!""" 
Luke23:38 A written notice was placed above him. It said, ""This is the king of the Jews.""" 
Luke23:39 One of the criminals hanging there insulted Jesus by saying, ""So you're really the Messiah, are you? Well, save yourself and us!""" 
Luke23:40 But the other criminal scolded him: Don't you fear God at all? Can't you see that you're condemned in the same way that he is? 
Luke23:41 Our punishment is fair. We're getting what we deserve. But this man hasn't done anything wrong. 
Luke23:42 Then he said, ""Jesus, remember me when you enter your kingdom.""" 
Luke23:43 Jesus said to him, ""I can guarantee this truth: Today you will be with me in paradise.""" 
Luke23:44 Around noon darkness came over the entire land and lasted until three in the afternoon. 
Luke23:45 The sun had stopped shining. The curtain in the temple was split in two. 
Luke23:46 Jesus cried out in a loud voice, ""Father, into your hands I entrust my spirit."" After he said this, he died." 
Luke23:47 When an army officer saw what had happened, he praised God and said, ""Certainly, this man was innocent!""" 
Luke23:48 Crowds had gathered to see the sight. But when all of them saw what had happened, they cried and returned to the city." 
Luke23:49 All his friends, including the women who had followed him from Galilee, stood at a distance and watched everything." 
Luke23:50 There was a good man who had God's approval. His name was Joseph. He was a member of the Jewish council," 
Luke23:51 but he had not agreed with what they had done. He was from the Jewish city of Arimathea, and he was waiting for the kingdom of God." 
Luke23:52 He went to Pilate and asked for the body of Jesus. 
Luke23:53 After he took it down from the cross, he wrapped it in linen. Then he laid the body in a tomb cut in rock, a tomb in which no one had ever been buried." 
Luke23:54 It was Friday, and the day of worship was just beginning." 
Luke23:55 The women who had come with Jesus from Galilee followed closely behind Joseph. They observed the tomb and how his body was laid in it. 
Luke23:56 Then they went back to the city and prepared spices and perfumes. But on the day of worship they rested according to the commandment. 
Luke24:1 Very early on Sunday morning the women went to the tomb. They were carrying the spices that they had prepared. 
Luke24:2 They found that the stone had been rolled away from the tomb. 
Luke24:3 When they went in, they did not find the body of the Lord Jesus." 
Luke24:4 While they were puzzled about this, two men in clothes that were as bright as lightning suddenly stood beside them." 
Luke24:5 The women were terrified and bowed to the ground. The men asked the women, ""Why are you looking among the dead for the living one?" 
Luke24:6 He's not here. He has been brought back to life! Remember what he told you while he was still in Galilee. 
Luke24:7 He said, 'The Son of Man must be handed over to sinful people, be crucified, and come back to life on the third day.' """ 
Luke24:8 Then the women remembered what Jesus had told them. 
Luke24:9 The women left the tomb and went back to the city. They told everything to the eleven apostles and all the others. 
Luke24:10 The women were Mary from Magdala, Joanna, and Mary (the mother of James). There were also other women with them. They told the apostles everything." 
Luke24:10 The women were Mary from Magdala, Joanna, and Mary (the mother of James). There were also other women with them. They told the apostles everything." 
Luke24:11 The apostles thought that the women's story didn't make any sense, and they didn't believe them." 
Luke24:12 But Peter got up and ran to the tomb. He bent down to look inside and saw only the strips of linen. Then he went away, wondering what had happened." 
Luke24:13 On the same day, two of Jesus' disciples were going to a village called Emmaus. It was about seven miles from Jerusalem." 
Luke24:14 They were talking to each other about everything that had happened. 
Luke24:15 While they were talking, Jesus approached them and began walking with them." 
Luke24:16 Although they saw him, they didn't recognize him." 
Luke24:17 He asked them, ""What are you discussing?"" They stopped and looked very sad." 
Luke24:18 One of them, Cleopas, replied, ""Are you the only one in Jerusalem who doesn't know what has happened recently?""" 
Luke24:19 What happened? he asked. They said to him, ""We were discussing what happened to Jesus from Nazareth. He was a powerful prophet in what he did and said in the sight of God and all the people." 
Luke24:20 Our chief priests and rulers had him condemned to death and crucified. 
Luke24:21 We were hoping that he was the one who would free Israel. What's more, this is now the third day since everything happened." 
Luke24:22 Some of the women from our group startled us. They went to the tomb early this morning 
Luke24:23 and didn't find his body. They told us that they had seen angels who said that he's alive. 
Luke24:24 Some of our men went to the tomb and found it empty, as the women had said, but they didn't see him.""" 
Luke24:25 Then Jesus said to them, ""How foolish you are! You're so slow to believe everything the prophets said!" 
Luke24:26 Didn't the Messiah have to suffer these things and enter into his glory? 
Luke24:27 Then he began with Moses' Teachings and the Prophets to explain to them what was said about him throughout the Scriptures. 
Luke24:28 When they came near the village where they were going, Jesus acted as if he were going farther." 
Luke24:29 They urged him, ""Stay with us! It's getting late, and the day is almost over."" So he went to stay with them." 
Luke24:30 While he was at the table with them, he took bread and blessed it. He broke the bread and gave it to them." 
Luke24:31 Then their eyes were opened, and they recognized him. But he vanished from their sight." 
Luke24:32 They said to each other, ""Weren't we excited when he talked with us on the road and opened up the meaning of the Scriptures for us?""" 
Luke24:33 That same hour they went back to Jerusalem. They found the eleven apostles and those who were with them gathered together. 
Luke24:34 They were saying, ""The Lord has really come back to life and has appeared to Simon.""" 
Luke24:35 Then the two disciples told what had happened on the road and how they had recognized Jesus when he broke the bread. 
Luke24:36 While they were talking about what had happened, Jesus stood among them. He said to them, ""Peace be with you!""" 
Luke24:37 They were terrified, and thought they were seeing a ghost." 
Luke24:38 He asked them, ""Why are you afraid? Why do you have doubts?" 
Luke24:39 Look at my hands and feet, and see that it's really me. Touch me, and see for yourselves. Ghosts don't have flesh and bones, but you can see that I do.""" 
Luke24:40 As he said this, he showed them his hands and feet." 
Luke24:41 The disciples were overcome with joy and amazement because this seemed too good to be true. Then Jesus asked them, ""Do you have anything to eat?""" 
Luke24:42 They gave him a piece of broiled fish. 
Luke24:43 He took it and ate it while they watched him. 
Luke24:44 Then he said to them, ""These are the words I spoke to you while I was still with you. I told you that everything written about me in Moses' Teachings, the Prophets, and the Psalms had to come true.""" 
Luke24:45 Then he opened their minds to understand the Scriptures. 
Luke24:46 He said to them, ""Scripture says that the Messiah would suffer and that he would come back to life on the third day." 
Luke24:47 Scripture also says that by the authority of Jesus people would be told to turn to God and change the way they think and act so that their sins will be forgiven. This would be told to people from all nations, beginning in the city of Jerusalem." 
Luke24:48 You are witnesses to these things. 
Luke24:49 I'm sending you what my Father promised. Wait here in the city until you receive power from heaven. 
Luke24:50 Then Jesus took them to a place near Bethany. There he raised his hands and blessed them. 
Luke24:51 While he was blessing them, he left them and was taken to heaven." 
Luke24:52 The disciples worshiped him and were overjoyed as they went back to Jerusalem. 
Luke24:53 They were always in the temple, where they praised God." 
John1:1 In the beginning the Word already existed. The Word was with God, and the Word was God." 
John1:2 He was already with God in the beginning. 
John1:3 Everything came into existence through him. Not one thing that exists was made without him. 
John1:4 He was the source of life, and that life was the light for humanity." 
John1:5 The light shines in the dark, and the dark has never extinguished it." 
John1:6 God sent a man named John to be his messenger. 
John1:7 John came to declare the truth about the light so that everyone would become believers through his message. 
John1:8 John was not the light, but he came to declare the truth about the light." 
John1:9 The real light, which shines on everyone, was coming into the world." 
John1:10 He was in the world, and the world came into existence through him. Yet, the world didn't recognize him." 
John1:11 He went to his own people, and his own people didn't accept him." 
John1:12 However, he gave the right to become God's children to everyone who believed in him." 
John1:13 These people didn't become God's children in a physical way—from a human impulse or from a husband's desire {to have a child}. They were born from God. 
John1:14 The Word became human and lived among us. We saw his glory. It was the glory that the Father shares with his only Son, a glory full of kindness and truth." 
John1:15 (John declared the truth about him when he said loudly, ""This is the person about whom I said, 'The one who comes after me was before me because he existed before I did.' "")" 
John1:16 Each of us has received one gift after another because of all that the Word is. 
John1:17 The Teachings were given through Moses, but kindness and truth came into existence through Jesus Christ." 
John1:18 No one has ever seen God. God's only Son, the one who is closest to the Father's heart, has made him known." 
John1:19 This was John's answer when the Jews sent priests and Levites from Jerusalem to ask him, ""Who are you?""" 
John1:20 John didn't refuse to answer. He told them clearly, ""I'm not the Messiah.""" 
John1:21 They asked him, ""Well, are you Elijah?"" John answered, ""No, I'm not."" Then they asked, ""Are you the prophet?"" John replied, ""No.""" 
John1:22 So they asked him, ""Who are you? Tell us so that we can take an answer back to those who sent us. What do you say about yourself?""" 
John1:23 John said, ""I'm a voice crying out in the desert, 'Make the way for the Lord straight,' as the prophet Isaiah said.""" 
John1:24 Some of those who had been sent were Pharisees. 
John1:25 They asked John, ""Why do you baptize if you're not the Messiah or Elijah or the prophet?""" 
John1:26 John answered them, ""I baptize with water. Someone you don't know is standing among you." 
John1:27 He's the one who comes after me. I am not worthy to untie his sandal strap. 
John1:28 This happened in Bethany on the east side of the Jordan River, where John was baptizing." 
John1:29 John saw Jesus coming toward him the next day and said, ""Look! This is the Lamb of God who takes away the sin of the world." 
John1:30 He is the one I spoke about when I said, 'A man who comes after me was before me because he existed before I did.'" 
John1:31 I didn't know who he was. However, I came to baptize with water to show him to the people of Israel.""" 
John1:32 John said, ""I saw the Spirit come down as a dove from heaven and stay on him." 
John1:33 I didn't know who he was. But God, who sent me to baptize with water, had told me, 'When you see the Spirit come down and stay on someone, you'll know that person is the one who baptizes with the Holy Spirit.'" 
John1:34 I have seen this and have declared that this is the Son of God. 
John1:35 The next day John was standing with two of his disciples. 
John1:36 John saw Jesus walk by. John said, ""Look! This is the Lamb of God.""" 
John1:37 When the two disciples heard John say this, they followed Jesus." 
John1:38 Jesus turned around and saw them following him. He asked them, ""What are you looking for?"" They said to him, ""Rabbi"" (which means ""teacher""), ""where are you staying?""" 
John1:39 Jesus told them, ""Come, and you will see."" So they went to see where he was staying and spent the rest of that day with him. It was about ten o'clock in the morning." 
John1:40 Andrew, Simon Peter's brother, was one of the two disciples who heard John and followed Jesus." 
John1:41 Andrew at once found his brother Simon and told him, ""We have found the Messiah"" (which means ""Christ"")." 
John1:42 Andrew brought Simon to Jesus. Jesus looked at Simon and said, ""You are Simon, son of John. Your name will be Cephas"" (which means ""Peter"")." 
John1:43 The next day Jesus wanted to go to Galilee. He found Philip and told him, ""Follow me!""" 
John1:44 (Philip was from Bethsaida, the hometown of Andrew and Peter.)" 
John1:45 Philip found Nathanael and told him, ""We have found the man whom Moses wrote about in his teachings and whom the prophets wrote about. He is Jesus, son of Joseph, from the city of Nazareth.""" 
John1:46 Nathanael said to Philip, ""Can anything good come from Nazareth?"" Philip told him, ""Come and see!""" 
John1:47 Jesus saw Nathanael coming toward him and remarked, ""Here is a true Israelite who is sincere.""" 
John1:48 Nathanael asked Jesus, ""How do you know anything about me?"" Jesus answered him, ""I saw you under the fig tree before Philip called you.""" 
John1:49 Nathanael said to Jesus, ""Rabbi, you are the Son of God! You are the king of Israel!""" 
John1:50 Jesus replied, ""You believe because I told you that I saw you under the fig tree. You will see greater things than that.""" 
John1:51 Jesus said to Nathanael, ""I can guarantee this truth: You will see the sky open and God's angels going up and coming down to the Son of Man.""" 
John2:1 Three days later a wedding took place in the city of Cana in Galilee. Jesus' mother was there. 
John2:2 Jesus and his disciples had been invited too. 
John2:3 When the wine was gone, Jesus' mother said to him, ""They're out of wine.""" 
John2:4 Jesus said to her, ""Why did you come to me? My time has not yet come.""" 
John2:5 His mother told the servers, ""Do whatever he tells you.""" 
John2:6 Six stone water jars were there. They were used for Jewish purification rituals. Each jar held 18 to 27 gallons. 
John2:7 Jesus told the servers, ""Fill the jars with water."" The servers filled the jars to the brim." 
John2:8 Jesus said to them, ""Pour some, and take it to the person in charge."" The servers did as they were told." 
John2:9 The person in charge tasted the water that had become wine. He didn't know where it had come from, although the servers who had poured the water knew. The person in charge called the groom" 
John2:10 and said to him, ""Everyone serves the best wine first. When people are drunk, the host serves cheap wine. But you have saved the best wine for now.""" 
John2:11 Cana in Galilee was the place where Jesus began to perform miracles. He made his glory public there, and his disciples believed in him." 
John2:12 After this, Jesus, his mother, brothers, and disciples went to the city of Capernaum and stayed there for a few days." 
John2:13 The Jewish Passover was near, so Jesus went to Jerusalem." 
John2:14 He found those who were selling cattle, sheep, and pigeons in the temple courtyard. He also found moneychangers sitting there." 
John2:15 He made a whip from small ropes and threw everyone with their sheep and cattle out of the temple courtyard. He dumped the moneychangers' coins and knocked over their tables. 
John2:16 He told those who sold pigeons, ""Pick up this stuff, and get it out of here! Stop making my Father's house a marketplace!""" 
John2:17 His disciples remembered that Scripture said, ""Devotion for your house will consume me.""" 
John2:18 The Jews reacted by asking Jesus, ""What miracle can you show us to justify what you're doing?""" 
John2:19 Jesus replied, ""Tear down this temple, and I'll rebuild it in three days.""" 
John2:20 The Jews said, ""It took forty-six years to build this temple. Do you really think you're going to rebuild it in three days?""" 
John2:21 But the temple Jesus spoke about was his own body. 
John2:22 After he came back to life, his disciples remembered that he had said this. So they believed the Scripture and this statement that Jesus had made." 
John2:23 While Jesus was in Jerusalem at the Passover festival, many people believed in him because they saw the miracles that he performed." 
John2:24 Jesus, however, was wary of these believers. He understood people" 
John2:25 and didn't need anyone to tell him about human nature. He knew what people were really like. 
John3:1 Nicodemus was a Pharisee and a member of the Jewish council. 
John3:2 He came to Jesus one night and said to him, ""Rabbi, we know that God has sent you as a teacher. No one can perform the miracles you perform unless God is with him.""" 
John3:3 Jesus replied to Nicodemus, ""I can guarantee this truth: No one can see the kingdom of God without being born from above.""" 
John3:4 Nicodemus asked him, ""How can anyone be born when he's an old man? He can't go back inside his mother a second time to be born, can he?""" 
John3:5 Jesus answered Nicodemus, ""I can guarantee this truth: No one can enter the kingdom of God without being born of water and the Spirit." 
John3:6 Flesh and blood give birth to flesh and blood, but the Spirit gives birth to things that are spiritual." 
John3:7 Don't be surprised when I tell you that all of you must be born from above. 
John3:8 The wind blows wherever it pleases. You hear its sound, but you don't know where the wind comes from or where it's going. That's the way it is with everyone born of the Spirit.""" 
John3:9 Nicodemus replied, ""How can that be?""" 
John3:10 Jesus told Nicodemus, ""You're a well-known teacher of Israel. Can't you understand this?" 
John3:11 I can guarantee this truth: We know what we're talking about, and we confirm what we've seen. Yet, you don't accept our message." 
John3:12 If you don't believe me when I tell you about things on earth, how will you believe me when I tell you about things in heaven?" 
John3:13 No one has gone to heaven except the Son of Man, who came from heaven." 
John3:14 As Moses lifted up the snake {on a pole} in the desert, so the Son of Man must be lifted up." 
John3:15 Then everyone who believes in him will have eternal life. 
John3:16 God loved the world this way: He gave his only Son so that everyone who believes in him will not die but will have eternal life. 
John3:17 God sent his Son into the world, not to condemn the world, but to save the world." 
John3:18 Those who believe in him won't be condemned. But those who don't believe are already condemned because they don't believe in God's only Son. 
John3:19 This is why people are condemned: The light came into the world. Yet, people loved the dark rather than the light because their actions were evil." 
John3:20 People who do what is wrong hate the light and don't come to the light. They don't want their actions to be exposed. 
John3:21 But people who do what is true come to the light so that the things they do for God may be clearly seen. 
John3:22 Later, Jesus and his disciples went to the Judean countryside, where he spent some time with them and baptized people." 
John3:22 Later, Jesus and his disciples went to the Judean countryside, where he spent some time with them and baptized people." 
John3:23 John was baptizing in Aenon, near Salim. Water was plentiful there. (People came to John to be baptized," 
John3:24 since John had not yet been put in prison.) 
John3:25 Some of John's disciples had an argument with a Jew about purification ceremonies. 
John3:26 So they went to John and asked him, ""Rabbi, do you remember the man you spoke so favorably about when he was with you on the other side of the Jordan River? Well, he's baptizing, and everyone is going to him!""" 
John3:27 John answered, ""People can't receive anything unless it has been given to them from heaven." 
John3:28 You are witnesses that I said, 'I'm not the Messiah, but I've been sent ahead of him.'" 
John3:29 The groom is the person to whom the bride belongs. The best man, who stands and listens to him, is overjoyed when the groom speaks. This is the joy that I feel." 
John3:30 He must increase in importance, while I must decrease in importance." 
John3:31 The person who comes from above is superior to everyone. I, a person from the earth, know nothing but what is on earth, and that's all I can talk about. The person who comes from heaven is superior to everyone" 
John3:32 and tells what he has seen and heard. Yet, no one accepts what he says." 
John3:33 I have accepted what that person said, and I have affirmed that God is truthful." 
John3:34 The man whom God has sent speaks God's message. After all, God gives him the Spirit without limit." 
John3:35 The Father loves his Son and has put everything in his power. 
John3:36 Whoever believes in the Son has eternal life, but whoever rejects the Son will not see life. Instead, he will see God's constant anger.""" 
John4:1 Jesus knew that the Pharisees had heard that he was making and baptizing more disciples than John. 
John4:2 (Actually, Jesus was not baptizing people. His disciples were.)" 
John4:3 So he left the Judean countryside and went back to Galilee. 
John4:3 So he left the Judean countryside and went back to Galilee. 
John4:4 Jesus had to go through Samaria. 
John4:5 He arrived at a city in Samaria called Sychar. Sychar was near the piece of land that Jacob had given to his son Joseph. 
John4:6 Jacob's Well was there. Jesus sat down by the well because he was tired from traveling. The time was about six o'clock in the evening. 
John4:7 A Samaritan woman went to get some water. Jesus said to her, ""Give me a drink of water.""" 
John4:8 (His disciples had gone into the city to buy some food.) 
John4:9 The Samaritan woman asked him, ""How can a Jewish man like you ask a Samaritan woman like me for a drink of water?"" (Jews, of course, don't associate with Samaritans.)" 
John4:10 Jesus replied to her, ""If you only knew what God's gift is and who is asking you for a drink, you would have asked him for a drink. He would have given you living water.""" 
John4:11 The woman said to him, ""Sir, you don't have anything to use to get water, and the well is deep. So where are you going to get this living water?" 
John4:12 You're not more important than our ancestor Jacob, are you? He gave us this well. He and his sons and his animals drank water from it.""" 
John4:13 Jesus answered her, ""Everyone who drinks this water will become thirsty again." 
John4:14 But those who drink the water that I will give them will never become thirsty again. In fact, the water I will give them will become in them a spring that gushes up to eternal life.""" 
John4:15 The woman told Jesus, ""Sir, give me this water! Then I won't get thirsty or have to come here to get water.""" 
John4:16 Jesus told her, ""Go to your husband, and bring him here.""" 
John4:17 The woman replied, ""I don't have a husband."" Jesus told her, ""You're right when you say that you don't have a husband." 
John4:18 You've had five husbands, and the man you have now isn't your husband. You've told the truth.""" 
John4:19 The woman said to Jesus, ""I see that you're a prophet!" 
John4:20 Our ancestors worshiped on this mountain. But you Jews say that people must worship in Jerusalem. 
John4:21 Jesus told her, ""Believe me. A time is coming when you Samaritans won't be worshiping the Father on this mountain or in Jerusalem." 
John4:22 You don't know what you're worshiping. We {Jews} know what we're worshiping, because salvation comes from the Jews." 
John4:23 Indeed, the time is coming, and it is now here, when the true worshipers will worship the Father in spirit and truth. The Father is looking for people like that to worship him." 
John4:24 God is a spirit. Those who worship him must worship in spirit and truth. 
John4:25 The woman said to him, ""I know that the Messiah is coming. When he comes, he will tell us everything."" (Messiah is the one called Christ.)" 
John4:26 Jesus told her, ""I am he, and I am speaking to you now.""" 
John4:27 At that time his disciples returned. They were surprised that he was talking to a woman. But none of them asked him, ""What do you want from her?"" or ""Why are you talking to her?""" 
John4:28 Then the woman left her water jar and went back into the city. She told the people," 
John4:29 Come with me, and meet a man who told me everything I've ever done. Could he be the Messiah?""" 
John4:30 The people left the city and went to meet Jesus. 
John4:31 Meanwhile, the disciples were urging him, ""Rabbi, have something to eat.""" 
John4:32 Jesus told them, ""I have food to eat that you don't know about.""" 
John4:33 The disciples asked each other, ""Did someone bring him something to eat?""" 
John4:34 Jesus told them, ""My food is to do what the one who sent me wants me to do and to finish the work he has given me." 
John4:35 Don't you say, 'In four more months the harvest will be here'? I'm telling you to look and see that the fields are ready to be harvested." 
John4:36 The person who harvests the crop is already getting paid. He is gathering grain for eternal life. So the person who plants the grain and the person who harvests it are happy together. 
John4:37 In this respect the saying is true: 'One person plants, and another person harvests.'" 
John4:38 I have sent you to harvest a crop you have not worked for. Other people have done the hard work, and you have followed them in their work.""" 
John4:39 Many Samaritans in that city believed in Jesus because of the woman who said, ""He told me everything I've ever done.""" 
John4:40 So when the Samaritans went to Jesus, they asked him to stay with them. He stayed in Samaria for two days." 
John4:41 Many more Samaritans believed because of what Jesus said. 
John4:42 They told the woman, ""Our faith is no longer based on what you've said. We have heard him ourselves, and we know that he really is the savior of the world.""" 
John4:43 After spending two days in Samaria, Jesus left for Galilee." 
John4:44 Jesus had said that a prophet is not honored in his own country. 
John4:45 But when Jesus arrived in Galilee, the people of Galilee welcomed him. They had seen everything he had done at the festival in Jerusalem, since they, too, had attended the festival." 
John4:46 Jesus returned to the city of Cana in Galilee, where he had changed water into wine. A government official was in Cana. His son was sick in Capernaum." 
John4:47 The official heard that Jesus had returned from Judea to Galilee. So he went to Jesus and asked him to go to Capernaum with him to heal his son who was about to die. 
John4:47 The official heard that Jesus had returned from Judea to Galilee. So he went to Jesus and asked him to go to Capernaum with him to heal his son who was about to die. 
John4:48 Jesus told the official, ""If people don't see miracles and amazing things, they won't believe.""" 
John4:49 The official said to him, ""Sir, come with me before my little boy dies.""" 
John4:50 Jesus told him, ""Go home. Your son will live."" The man believed what Jesus told him and left." 
John4:51 While the official was on his way to Capernaum, his servants met him and told him that his boy was alive." 
John4:52 The official asked them at what time his son got better. His servants told him, ""The fever left him yesterday evening at seven o'clock.""" 
John4:53 Then the boy's father realized that it was the same time that Jesus had told him, ""Your son will live."" So the official and his entire family became believers." 
John4:54 This was the second miracle that Jesus performed after he had come back from Judea to Galilee. 
John4:54 This was the second miracle that Jesus performed after he had come back from Judea to Galilee. 
John5:1 Later, Jesus went to Jerusalem for a Jewish festival." 
John5:2 Near Sheep Gate in Jerusalem was a pool called Bethesda in Hebrew. It had five porches. 
John5:3 Under these porches a large number of sick people—people who were blind, lame, or paralyzed—used to lie." 
John5:4 *** 
John5:5 One man, who had been sick for 38 years, was lying there." 
John5:6 Jesus saw the man lying there and knew that he had been sick for a long time. So Jesus asked the man, ""Would you like to get well?""" 
John5:7 The sick man answered Jesus, ""Sir, I don't have anyone to put me into the pool when the water is stirred. While I'm trying to get there, someone else steps into the pool ahead of me.""" 
John5:8 Jesus told the man, ""Get up, pick up your cot, and walk.""" 
John5:9 The man immediately became well, picked up his cot, and walked. That happened on a day of worship." 
John5:10 So the Jews told the man who had been healed, ""This is a day of worship. You're not allowed to carry your cot today.""" 
John5:11 The man replied, ""The man who made me well told me to pick up my cot and walk.""" 
John5:12 The Jews asked him, ""Who is the man who told you to pick it up and walk?""" 
John5:13 But the man who had been healed didn't know who Jesus was. (Jesus had withdrawn from the crowd.) 
John5:14 Later, Jesus met the man in the temple courtyard and told him, ""You're well now. Stop sinning so that something worse doesn't happen to you.""" 
John5:15 The man went back to the Jews and told them that Jesus was the man who had made him well. 
John5:16 The Jews began to persecute Jesus because he kept healing people on the day of worship. 
John5:17 Jesus replied to them, ""My Father is working right now, and so am I.""" 
John5:18 His reply made the Jews more intent on killing him. Not only did he break the laws about the day of worship, but also he made himself equal to God when he said repeatedly that God was his Father." 
John5:19 Jesus said to the Jews, ""I can guarantee this truth: The Son cannot do anything on his own. He can do only what he sees the Father doing. Indeed, the Son does exactly what the Father does." 
John5:20 The Father loves the Son and shows him everything he is doing. The Father will show him even greater things to do than these things so that you will be amazed. 
John5:21 In the same way that the Father brings back the dead and gives them life, the Son gives life to anyone he chooses." 
John5:22 The Father doesn't judge anyone. He has entrusted judgment entirely to the Son 
John5:23 so that everyone will honor the Son as they honor the Father. Whoever doesn't honor the Son doesn't honor the Father who sent him. 
John5:24 I can guarantee this truth: Those who listen to what I say and believe in the one who sent me will have eternal life. They won't be judged because they have already passed from death to life. 
John5:25 I can guarantee this truth: A time is coming (and is now here) when the dead will hear the voice of the Son of God and those who respond to it will live. 
John5:26 The Father is the source of life, and he has enabled the Son to be the source of life too." 
John5:27 He has also given the Son authority to pass judgment because he is the Son of Man. 
John5:28 Don't be surprised at what I've just said. A time is coming when all the dead will hear his voice," 
John5:29 and they will come out of their tombs. Those who have done good will come back to life and live. But those who have done evil will come back to life and will be judged. 
John5:30 I can't do anything on my own. As I listen {to the Father}, I make my judgments. My judgments are right because I don't try to do what I want but what the one who sent me wants." 
John5:31 If I testify on my own behalf, what I say isn't true." 
John5:32 Someone else testifies on my behalf, and I know that what he says about me is true." 
John5:33 You sent people to John {the Baptizer}, and he testified to the truth." 
John5:34 But I don't depend on human testimony. I'm telling you this to save you. 
John5:35 John was a lamp that gave off brilliant light. For a time you enjoyed the pleasure of his light. 
John5:36 But I have something that testifies more favorably on my behalf than John's testimony. The tasks that the Father gave me to carry out, these tasks which I perform, testify on my behalf. They prove that the Father has sent me." 
John5:37 The Father who sent me testifies on my behalf. You have never heard his voice, and you have never seen his form." 
John5:38 So you don't have the Father's message within you, because you don't believe in the person he has sent." 
John5:39 You study the Scriptures in detail because you think you have the source of eternal life in them. These Scriptures testify on my behalf. 
John5:40 Yet, you don't want to come to me to get {eternal} life." 
John5:41 I don't accept praise from humans. 
John5:42 But I know what kind of people you are. You don't have any love for God. 
John5:43 I have come with the authority my Father has given me, but you don't accept me. If someone else comes with his own authority, you will accept him." 
John5:44 How can you believe when you accept each other's praise and don't look for the praise that comes from the only God? 
John5:45 Don't think that I will accuse you in the presence of the Father. Moses, the one you trust, is already accusing you." 
John5:46 If you really believed Moses, you would believe me. Moses wrote about me." 
John5:47 If you don't believe what Moses wrote, how will you ever believe what I say?""" 
John6:1 Jesus later crossed to the other side of the Sea of Galilee (or the Sea of Tiberias). 
John6:2 A large crowd followed him because they saw the miracles that he performed for the sick. 
John6:3 Jesus went up a mountain and sat with his disciples. 
John6:4 The time for the Jewish Passover festival was near. 
John6:5 As Jesus saw a large crowd coming to him, he said to Philip, ""Where can we buy bread for these people to eat?""" 
John6:6 Jesus asked this question to test him. He already knew what he was going to do. 
John6:7 Philip answered, ""We would need about a year's wages to buy enough bread for each of them to have a piece.""" 
John6:8 One of Jesus' disciples, Andrew, who was Simon Peter's brother, told him," 
John6:9 A boy who has five loaves of barley bread and two small fish is here. But they won't go very far for so many people. 
John6:10 Jesus said, ""Have the people sit down."" The people had plenty of grass to sit on. (There were about 5,000 men in the crowd.)" 
John6:11 Jesus took the loaves, gave thanks, and distributed them to the people who were sitting there. He did the same thing with the fish. All the people ate as much as they wanted." 
John6:12 When the people were full, Jesus told his disciples, ""Gather the leftover pieces so that nothing will be wasted.""" 
John6:13 The disciples gathered the leftover pieces of bread and filled twelve baskets. 
John6:14 When the people saw the miracle Jesus performed, they said, ""This man is certainly the prophet who is to come into the world.""" 
John6:15 Jesus realized that the people intended to take him by force and make him king. So he returned to the mountain by himself. 
John6:16 When evening came, his disciples went to the sea." 
John6:17 They got into a boat and started to cross the sea to the city of Capernaum. By this time it was dark, and Jesus had not yet come to them." 
John6:18 A strong wind started to blow and stir up the sea. 
John6:19 After they had rowed three or four miles, they saw Jesus walking on the sea. He was coming near the boat, and they became terrified." 
John6:20 Jesus told them, ""It's me. Don't be afraid!""" 
John6:21 So they were willing to help Jesus into the boat. Immediately, the boat reached the shore where they were going." 
John6:22 On the next day the people were still on the other side of the sea. They noticed that only one boat was there and that Jesus had not stepped into that boat with his disciples. The disciples had gone away without him. 
John6:23 Other boats from Tiberias arrived near the place where they had eaten the bread after the Lord gave thanks. 
John6:24 When the people saw that neither Jesus nor his disciples were there, they got into these boats and went to the city of Capernaum to look for Jesus." 
John6:25 When they found him on the other side of the sea, they asked him, ""Rabbi, when did you get here?""" 
John6:26 Jesus replied to them, ""I can guarantee this truth: You're not looking for me because you saw miracles. You are looking for me because you ate as much of those loaves as you wanted." 
John6:27 Don't work for food that spoils. Instead, work for the food that lasts into eternal life. This is the food the Son of Man will give you. After all, the Father has placed his seal of approval on him.""" 
John6:28 The people asked Jesus, ""What does God want us to do?""" 
John6:29 Jesus replied to them, ""God wants to do something for you so that you believe in the one whom he has sent.""" 
John6:30 The people asked him, ""What miracle are you going to perform so that we can see it and believe in you? What are you going to do?" 
John6:31 Our ancestors ate the manna in the desert. Scripture says, 'He gave them bread from heaven to eat.' """ 
John6:32 Jesus said to them, ""I can guarantee this truth: Moses didn't give you bread from heaven, but my Father gives you the true bread from heaven." 
John6:33 God's bread is the man who comes from heaven and gives life to the world. 
John6:34 They said to him, ""Sir, give us this bread all the time.""" 
John6:35 Jesus told them, ""I am the bread of life. Whoever comes to me will never become hungry, and whoever believes in me will never become thirsty." 
John6:36 I've told you that you have seen me. However, you don't believe in me." 
John6:37 Everyone whom the Father gives me will come to me. I will never turn away anyone who comes to me. 
John6:38 I haven't come from heaven to do what I want to do. I've come to do what the one who sent me wants me to do. 
John6:39 The one who sent me doesn't want me to lose any of those he gave me. He wants me to bring them back to life on the last day. 
John6:40 My Father wants all those who see the Son and believe in him to have eternal life. He wants me to bring them back to life on the last day. 
John6:41 The Jews began to criticize Jesus for saying, ""I am the bread that came from heaven.""" 
John6:42 They asked, ""Isn't this man Jesus, Joseph's son? Don't we know his father and mother? How can he say now, 'I came from heaven'?""" 
John6:43 Jesus responded, ""Stop criticizing me!" 
John6:44 People cannot come to me unless the Father who sent me brings them to me. I will bring these people back to life on the last day. 
John6:45 The prophets wrote, 'God will teach everyone.' Those who do what they have learned from the Father come to me." 
John6:46 I'm saying that no one has seen the Father. Only the one who is from God has seen the Father. 
John6:47 I can guarantee this truth: Every believer has eternal life. 
John6:48 I am the bread of life. 
John6:49 Your ancestors ate the manna in the desert and died. 
John6:50 This is the bread that comes from heaven so that whoever eats it won't die. 
John6:51 I am the living bread that came from heaven. Whoever eats this bread will live forever. The bread I will give to bring life to the world is my flesh. 
John6:52 The Jews began to quarrel with each other. They said, ""How can this man give us his flesh to eat?""" 
John6:53 Jesus told them, ""I can guarantee this truth: If you don't eat the flesh of the Son of Man and drink his blood, you don't have the source of life in you." 
John6:54 Those who eat my flesh and drink my blood have eternal life, and I will bring them back to life on the last day." 
John6:55 My flesh is true food, and my blood is true drink." 
John6:56 Those who eat my flesh and drink my blood live in me, and I live in them." 
John6:57 The Father who has life sent me, and I live because of the Father. So those who feed on me will live because of me." 
John6:58 This is the bread that came from heaven. It is not like the bread your ancestors ate. They eventually died. Those who eat this bread will live forever. 
John6:59 Jesus said this while he was teaching in a synagogue in Capernaum. 
John6:60 When many of Jesus' disciples heard him, they said, ""What he says is hard to accept. Who wants to listen to him anymore?""" 
John6:61 Jesus was aware that his disciples were criticizing his message. So Jesus asked them, ""Did what I say make you lose faith?" 
John6:62 What if you see the Son of Man go where he was before? 
John6:63 Life is spiritual. Your physical existence doesn't contribute to that life. The words that I have spoken to you are spiritual. They are life. 
John6:64 But some of you don't believe. Jesus knew from the beginning those who wouldn't believe and the one who would betray him. 
John6:65 So he added, ""That is why I told you that people cannot come to me unless the Father provides the way.""" 
John6:66 Jesus' speech made many of his disciples go back to the lives they had led before they followed Jesus. 
John6:67 So Jesus asked the twelve apostles, ""Do you want to leave me too?""" 
John6:68 Simon Peter answered Jesus, ""Lord, to what person could we go? Your words give eternal life." 
John6:69 Besides, we believe and know that you are the Holy One of God.""" 
John6:70 Jesus replied, ""I chose all twelve of you. Yet, one of you is a devil.""" 
John6:71 Jesus meant Judas, son of Simon Iscariot. Judas, who was one of the twelve apostles, would later betray Jesus." 
John7:1 Jesus later traveled throughout Galilee. He didn't want to travel in Judea because Jews there wanted to kill him. 
John7:1 Jesus later traveled throughout Galilee. He didn't want to travel in Judea because Jews there wanted to kill him. 
John7:2 The time for the Jewish Festival of Booths was near. 
John7:3 So Jesus' brothers told him, ""Leave this place, and go to Judea so that your disciples can see the things that you're doing." 
John7:3 So Jesus' brothers told him, ""Leave this place, and go to Judea so that your disciples can see the things that you're doing." 
John7:4 No one does things secretly when he wants to be known publicly. If you do these things, you should let the world see you.""" 
John7:5 Even his brothers didn't believe in him. 
John7:6 Jesus told them, ""Now is not the right time for me to go. Any time is right for you." 
John7:7 The world cannot hate you, but it hates me because I say that what everyone does is evil." 
John7:8 Go to the festival. I'm not going to this festival right now. Now is not the right time for me to go. 
John7:9 After saying this, Jesus stayed in Galilee." 
John7:10 But after his brothers had gone to the festival, Jesus went. He didn't go publicly but secretly." 
John7:11 The Jews were looking for Jesus in the crowd at the festival. They kept asking, ""Where is that man?""" 
John7:12 The crowds argued about Jesus. Some people said, ""He's a good man,"" while others said, ""No he isn't. He deceives the people.""" 
John7:13 Yet, no one would talk openly about him because they were afraid of the Jews." 
John7:14 When the festival was half over, Jesus went to the temple courtyard and began to teach." 
John7:15 The Jews were surprised and asked, ""How can this man be so educated when he hasn't gone to school?""" 
John7:16 Jesus responded to them, ""What I teach doesn't come from me but from the one who sent me." 
John7:17 Those who want to follow the will of God will know if what I teach is from God or if I teach my own thoughts. 
John7:18 Those who speak their own thoughts are looking for their own glory. But the man who wants to bring glory to the one who sent him is a true teacher and doesn't have dishonest motives. 
John7:19 Didn't Moses give you his teachings? Yet, none of you does what Moses taught you. So why do you want to kill me?""" 
John7:20 The crowd answered, ""You're possessed by a demon! Who wants to kill you?""" 
John7:21 Jesus answered them, ""I performed one miracle, and all of you are surprised by it." 
John7:22 Moses gave you the teaching about circumcision (although it didn't come from Moses but from our ancestors). So you circumcise a male on a day of worship. 
John7:23 If you circumcise a male on the day of worship to follow Moses' Teachings, why are you angry with me because I made a man entirely well on the day of worship?" 
John7:24 Stop judging by outward appearance! Instead, judge correctly.""" 
John7:25 Some of the people who lived in Jerusalem said, ""Isn't this the man they want to kill?" 
John7:26 But look at this! He's speaking in public, and no one is saying anything to him! Can it be that the rulers really know that this man is the Messiah?" 
John7:27 However, we know where this man comes from. When the Christ comes, no one will know where he is from.""" 
John7:28 Then, while Jesus was teaching in the temple courtyard, he said loudly, ""You know me, and you know where I come from. I didn't decide to come on my own. The one who sent me is true. He's the one you don't know." 
John7:29 I know him because I am from him and he sent me. 
John7:30 The Jews tried to arrest him but couldn't because his time had not yet come. 
John7:31 However, many people in the crowd believed in him. They asked, ""When the Messiah comes, will he perform more miracles than this man has?""" 
John7:32 The Pharisees heard the crowd saying things like this about him. So the chief priests and the Pharisees sent temple guards to arrest Jesus. 
John7:33 Jesus said, ""I will still be with you for a little while. Then I'll go to the one who sent me." 
John7:34 You will look for me, but you won't find me. You can't go where I'm going.""" 
John7:35 The Jews said among themselves, ""Where does this man intend to go so that we won't find him? Does he mean that he'll live with the Jews who are scattered among the Greeks and that he'll teach the Greeks?" 
John7:36 What does he mean when he says, 'You will look for me, but you won't find me,' and 'You can't go where I'm going'?""" 
John7:37 On the last and most important day of the festival, Jesus was standing {in the temple courtyard}. He said loudly, ""Whoever is thirsty must come to me to drink." 
John7:38 As Scripture says, 'Streams of living water will flow from deep within the person who believes in me.' """ 
John7:39 Jesus said this about the Spirit, whom his believers would receive. The Spirit was not yet evident, as it would be after Jesus had been glorified." 
John7:40 After some of the crowd heard Jesus say these words, they said, ""This man is certainly the prophet.""" 
John7:41 Other people said, ""This man is the Messiah."" Still other people asked, ""How can the Messiah come from Galilee?" 
John7:42 Doesn't Scripture say that the Messiah will come from the descendants of David and from the village of Bethlehem, where David lived?""" 
John7:43 So the people were divided because of Jesus. 
John7:44 Some of them wanted to arrest him, but they couldn't." 
John7:45 When the temple guards returned, the chief priests and Pharisees asked them, ""Why didn't you bring Jesus?""" 
John7:46 The temple guards answered, ""No human has ever spoken like this man.""" 
John7:47 The Pharisees asked the temple guards, ""Have you been deceived too?" 
John7:48 Has any ruler or any Pharisee believed in him? 
John7:49 This crowd is cursed because it doesn't know Moses' Teachings. 
John7:50 One of those Pharisees was Nicodemus, who had previously visited Jesus. Nicodemus asked them," 
John7:51 Do Moses' Teachings enable us to judge a person without first hearing that person's side of the story? We can't judge a person without finding out what that person has done. 
John7:52 They asked Nicodemus, ""Are you saying this because you're from Galilee? Study {the Scriptures}, and you'll see that no prophet comes from Galilee.""" 
John7:53 Then each of them went home. 
John8:1 Jesus went to the Mount of Olives. 
John8:2 Early the next morning he returned to the temple courtyard. All the people went to him, so he sat down and began to teach them." 
John8:3 The scribes and the Pharisees brought a woman who had been caught committing adultery. They made her stand in front of everyone 
John8:4 and asked Jesus, ""Teacher, we caught this woman in the act of adultery." 
John8:5 In his teachings, Moses ordered us to stone women like this to death. What do you say?""" 
John8:6 They asked this to test him. They wanted to find a reason to bring charges against him. Jesus bent down and used his finger to write on the ground. 
John8:7 When they persisted in asking him questions, he straightened up and said, ""The person who is sinless should be the first to throw a stone at her.""" 
John8:8 Then he bent down again and continued writing on the ground. 
John8:9 One by one, beginning with the older men, the scribes and Pharisees left. Jesus was left alone with the woman." 
John8:10 Then Jesus straightened up and asked her, ""Where did they go? Has anyone condemned you?""" 
John8:11 The woman answered, ""No one, sir."" Jesus said, ""I don't condemn you either. Go! From now on don't sin.""" 
John8:12 Jesus spoke to the Pharisees again. He said, ""I am the light of the world. Whoever follows me will have a life filled with light and will never live in the dark.""" 
John8:13 The Pharisees said to him, ""You testify on your own behalf, so your testimony isn't true.""" 
John8:14 Jesus replied to them, ""Even if I testify on my own behalf, my testimony is true because I know where I came from and where I'm going. However, you don't know where I came from or where I'm going." 
John8:15 You judge the way humans do. I don't judge anyone. 
John8:16 Even if I do judge, my judgment is valid because I don't make it on my own. I make my judgment with the Father who sent me." 
John8:17 Your own teachings say that the testimony of two people is true. 
John8:18 I testify on my own behalf, and so does the Father who sent me.""" 
John8:19 The Pharisees asked him, ""Where is your father?"" Jesus replied, ""You don't know me or my Father. If you knew me, you would also know my Father.""" 
John8:20 Jesus spoke these words while he was teaching in the treasury area of the temple courtyard. No one arrested him, because his time had not yet come." 
John8:21 Jesus spoke to the Pharisees again. He said, ""I'm going away, and you'll look for me. But you will die because of your sin. You can't go where I'm going.""" 
John8:22 Then the Jews asked, ""Is he going to kill himself? Is that what he means when he says, 'You can't go where I'm going'?""" 
John8:23 Jesus said to them, ""You're from below. I'm from above. You're from this world. I'm not from this world." 
John8:24 For this reason I told you that you'll die because of your sins. If you don't believe that I am the one, you'll die because of your sins.""" 
John8:25 The Jews asked him, ""Who did you say you are?"" Jesus told them, ""I am who I said I was from the beginning." 
John8:26 I have a lot I could say about you and a lot I could condemn you for. But the one who sent me is true. So I tell the world exactly what he has told me. 
John8:27 (The Jews didn't know that he was talking to them about the Father.) 
John8:28 So Jesus told them, ""When you have lifted up the Son of Man, then you'll know that I am the one and that I can't do anything on my own. Instead, I speak as the Father taught me." 
John8:29 Besides, the one who sent me is with me. He hasn't left me by myself. I always do what pleases him.""" 
John8:30 As Jesus was saying this, many people believed in him." 
John8:31 So Jesus said to those Jews who believed in him, ""If you live by what I say, you are truly my disciples." 
John8:32 You will know the truth, and the truth will set you free.""" 
John8:33 They replied to Jesus, ""We are Abraham's descendants, and we've never been anyone's slaves. So how can you say that we will be set free?""" 
John8:34 Jesus answered them, ""I can guarantee this truth: Whoever lives a sinful life is a slave to sin." 
John8:35 A slave doesn't live in the home forever, but a son does." 
John8:36 So if the Son sets you free, you will be absolutely free." 
John8:37 I know that you're Abraham's descendants. However, you want to kill me because you don't like what I'm saying." 
John8:38 What I'm saying is what I have seen in my Father's presence. But you do what you've heard from your father. 
John8:39 The Jews replied to Jesus, ""Abraham is our father."" Jesus told them, ""If you were Abraham's children, you would do what Abraham did." 
John8:40 I am a man who has told you the truth that I heard from God. But now you want to kill me. Abraham wouldn't have done that. 
John8:41 You're doing what your father does. The Jews said to Jesus, ""We're not illegitimate children. God is our only Father.""" 
John8:42 Jesus told them, ""If God were your Father, you would love me. After all, I'm here, and I came from God. I didn't come on my own. Instead, God sent me." 
John8:43 Why don't you understand the language I use? Is it because you can't understand the words I use? 
John8:44 You come from your father, the devil, and you desire to do what your father wants you to do. The devil was a murderer from the beginning. He has never been truthful. He doesn't know what the truth is. Whenever he tells a lie, he's doing what comes naturally to him. He's a liar and the father of lies." 
John8:45 So you don't believe me because I tell the truth. 
John8:46 Can any of you convict me of committing a sin? If I'm telling the truth, why don't you believe me?" 
John8:47 The person who belongs to God understands what God says. You don't understand because you don't belong to God. 
John8:48 The Jews replied to Jesus, ""Aren't we right when we say that you're a Samaritan and that you're possessed by a demon?""" 
John8:49 Jesus answered, ""I'm not possessed. I honor my Father, but you dishonor me." 
John8:50 I don't want my own glory. But there is someone who wants it, and he is the judge." 
John8:51 I can guarantee this truth: Whoever obeys what I say will never see death. 
John8:52 The Jews told Jesus, ""Now we know that you're possessed by a demon. Abraham died, and so did the prophets, but you say, 'Whoever does what I say will never taste death.'" 
John8:53 Are you greater than our father Abraham, who died? The prophets have also died. Who do you think you are?""" 
John8:54 Jesus said, ""If I bring glory to myself, my glory is nothing. My Father is the one who gives me glory, and you say that he is your God." 
John8:55 Yet, you haven't known him. However, I know him. If I would say that I didn't know him, I would be a liar like all of you. But I do know him, and I do what he says." 
John8:56 Your father Abraham was pleased to see that my day was coming. He saw it and was happy. 
John8:57 The Jews said to Jesus, ""You're not even fifty years old. How could you have seen Abraham?""" 
John8:58 Jesus told them, ""I can guarantee this truth: Before Abraham was ever born, I am.""" 
John8:59 Then some of the Jews picked up stones to throw at Jesus. However, Jesus was concealed, and he left the temple courtyard." 
John9:1 As Jesus walked along, he saw a man who had been born blind." 
John9:2 His disciples asked him, ""Rabbi, why was this man born blind? Did he or his parents sin?""" 
John9:3 Jesus answered, ""Neither this man nor his parents sinned. Instead, he was born blind so that God could show what he can do for him." 
John9:4 We must do what the one who sent me wants us to do while it is day. The night when no one can do anything is coming. 
John9:5 As long as I'm in the world, I'm light for the world.""" 
John9:6 After Jesus said this, he spit on the ground and mixed the spit with dirt. Then he smeared it on the man's eyes" 
John9:7 and told him, ""Wash it off in the pool of Siloam."" (Siloam means ""sent."") The blind man washed it off and returned. He was able to see." 
John9:8 His neighbors and those who had previously seen him begging asked, ""Isn't this the man who used to sit and beg?""" 
John9:9 Some of them said, ""He's the one."" Others said, ""No, he isn't, but he looks like him."" But the man himself said, ""I am the one.""" 
John9:10 So they asked him, ""How did you receive your sight?""" 
John9:11 He replied, ""The man people call Jesus mixed some spit with dirt, smeared it on my eyes, and told me, 'Go to Siloam, and wash it off.' So I went there, washed it off, and received my sight.""" 
John9:12 They asked him, ""Where is that man?"" The man answered, ""I don't know.""" 
John9:13 Some people brought the man who had been blind to the Pharisees. 
John9:14 The day when Jesus mixed the spit and dirt and gave the man sight was a day of worship. 
John9:15 So the Pharisees asked the man again how he received his sight. The man told the Pharisees, ""He put a mixture of spit and dirt on my eyes. I washed it off, and now I can see.""" 
John9:16 Some of the Pharisees said, ""The man who did this is not from God because he doesn't follow the traditions for the day of worship."" Other Pharisees asked, ""How can a man who is a sinner perform miracles like these?"" So the Pharisees were divided in their opinions." 
John9:17 They asked the man who had been born blind another question: What do you say about the man who gave you sight? The man answered, ""He's a prophet.""" 
John9:18 Until they talked to the man's parents, the Jews didn't believe that the man had been blind and had been given sight." 
John9:19 They asked his parents, ""Is this your son, the one you say was born blind? Why can he see now?""" 
John9:20 His parents replied, ""We know that he's our son and that he was born blind." 
John9:21 But we don't know how he got his sight or who gave it to him. You'll have to ask him. He's old enough to answer for himself. 
John9:22 (His parents said this because they were afraid of the Jews. The Jews had already agreed to put anyone who acknowledged that Jesus was the Christ out of the synagogue. 
John9:23 That's why his parents said, ""You'll have to ask him. He's old enough."")" 
John9:24 So once again the Jews called the man who had been blind. They told him, ""Give glory to God. We know that this man who gave you sight is a sinner.""" 
John9:25 The man responded, ""I don't know if he's a sinner or not. But I do know one thing. I used to be blind, but now I can see.""" 
John9:26 The Jews asked him, ""What did he do to you? How did he give you sight?""" 
John9:27 The man replied, ""I've already told you, but you didn't listen. Why do you want to hear the story again? Do you want to become his disciples too?""" 
John9:28 The Jews yelled at him, ""You're his disciple, but we're Moses' disciples." 
John9:29 We know that God spoke to Moses, but we don't know where this man came from.""" 
John9:30 The man replied to them, ""That's amazing! You don't know where he's from. Yet, he gave me sight." 
John9:31 We know that God doesn't listen to sinners. Instead, he listens to people who are devout and who do what he wants." 
John9:32 Since the beginning of time, no one has ever heard of anyone giving sight to a person born blind." 
John9:33 If this man were not from God, he couldn't do anything like that.""" 
John9:34 The Jews answered him, ""You were born full of sin. Do you think you can teach us?"" Then they threw him out {of the synagogue}." 
John9:35 Jesus heard that the Jews had thrown the man out {of the synagogue}. So when Jesus found the man, he asked him, ""Do you believe in the Son of Man?""" 
John9:36 The man replied, ""Sir, tell me who he is so that I can believe in him.""" 
John9:37 Jesus told him, ""You've seen him. He is the person who is now talking with you.""" 
John9:38 The man bowed in front of Jesus and said, ""I believe, Lord.""" 
John9:39 Then Jesus said, ""I have come into this world to judge: Blind people will be given sight, and those who can see will become blind.""" 
John9:40 Some Pharisees who were with Jesus heard this. So they asked him, ""Do you think we're blind?""" 
John9:41 Jesus told them, ""If you were blind, you wouldn't be sinners. But now you say, 'We see,' so you continue to be sinners." 
John10:1 I can guarantee this truth: The person who doesn't enter the sheep pen through the gate but climbs in somewhere else is a thief or a robber. 
John10:2 But the one who enters through the gate is the shepherd. 
John10:3 The gatekeeper opens the gate for him, and the sheep respond to his voice. He calls his sheep by name and leads them out of the pen." 
John10:4 After he has brought out all his sheep, he walks ahead of them. The sheep follow him because they recognize his voice." 
John10:5 They won't follow a stranger. Instead, they will run away from a stranger because they don't recognize his voice.""" 
John10:6 Jesus used this illustration as he talked to the people, but they didn't understand what he meant." 
John10:7 Jesus emphasized, ""I can guarantee this truth: I am the gate for the sheep." 
John10:8 All who came before I did were thieves or robbers. However, the sheep didn't respond to them." 
John10:9 I am the gate. Those who enter the sheep pen through me will be saved. They will go in and out of the sheep pen and find food. 
John10:10 A thief comes to steal, kill, and destroy. But I came so that my sheep will have life and so that they will have everything they need." 
John10:11 I am the good shepherd. The good shepherd gives his life for the sheep. 
John10:12 A hired hand isn't a shepherd and doesn't own the sheep. When he sees a wolf coming, he abandons the sheep and quickly runs away. So the wolf drags the sheep away and scatters the flock." 
John10:13 The hired hand is concerned about what he's going to get paid and not about the sheep. 
John10:14 I am the good shepherd. I know my sheep as the Father knows me. My sheep know me as I know the Father. 
John10:15 So I give my life for my sheep. 
John10:16 I also have other sheep that are not from this pen. I must lead them. They, too, will respond to my voice. So they will be one flock with one shepherd." 
John10:17 The Father loves me because I give my life in order to take it back again. 
John10:18 No one takes my life from me. I give my life of my own free will. I have the authority to give my life, and I have the authority to take my life back again. This is what my Father ordered me to do.""" 
John10:19 The Jews were divided because of what Jesus said. 
John10:20 Many of them said, ""He's possessed by a demon! He's crazy! Why do you listen to him?""" 
John10:21 Others said, ""No one talks like this if he's possessed by a demon. Can a demon give sight to the blind?""" 
John10:22 The Festival of the Dedication of the Temple took place in Jerusalem during the winter. 
John10:23 Jesus was walking on Solomon's porch in the temple courtyard. 
John10:24 The Jews surrounded him. They asked him, ""How long will you keep us in suspense? If you are the Messiah, tell us plainly.""" 
John10:25 Jesus answered them, ""I've told you, but you don't believe me. The things that I do in my Father's name testify on my behalf." 
John10:26 However, you don't believe because you're not my sheep." 
John10:27 My sheep respond to my voice, and I know who they are. They follow me," 
John10:28 and I give them eternal life. They will never be lost, and no one will tear them away from me." 
John10:29 My Father, who gave them to me, is greater than everyone else, and no one can tear them away from my Father." 
John10:30 The Father and I are one. 
John10:31 The Jews had again brought some rocks to stone Jesus to death. 
John10:32 Jesus replied to them, ""I've shown you many good things that come from the Father. For which of these good things do you want to stone me to death?""" 
John10:33 The Jews answered Jesus, ""We're going to stone you to death, not for any good things you've done, but for dishonoring God. You claim to be God, although you're only a man.""" 
John10:34 Jesus said to them, ""Don't your Scriptures say, 'I said, ""You are gods"" '?" 
John10:35 The Scriptures cannot be discredited. So if God calls people gods (and they are the people to whom he gave the Scriptures)," 
John10:36 why do you say that I'm dishonoring God because I said, 'I'm the Son of God'? God set me apart for this holy purpose and has sent me into the world." 
John10:37 If I'm not doing the things my Father does, don't believe me." 
John10:38 But if I'm doing those things and you refuse to believe me, then at least believe the things that I'm doing. Then you will know and recognize that the Father is in me and that I am in the Father.""" 
John10:39 The Jews tried to arrest Jesus again, but he got away from them." 
John10:40 He went back across the Jordan River and stayed in the place where John first baptized people. 
John10:41 Many people went to Jesus. They said, ""John didn't perform any miracles, but everything John said about this man is true.""" 
John10:42 Many people there believed in Jesus. 
John11:1 Lazarus, who lived in Bethany, the village where Mary and her sister Martha lived, was sick." 
John11:2 (Mary was the woman who poured perfume on the Lord and wiped his feet with her hair. Her brother Lazarus was the one who was sick.) 
John11:3 So the sisters sent a messenger to tell Jesus, ""Lord, your close friend is sick.""" 
John11:4 When Jesus heard the message, he said, ""His sickness won't result in death. Instead, this sickness will bring glory to God so that the Son of God will receive glory through it.""" 
John11:5 Jesus loved Martha, her sister, and Lazarus." 
John11:6 Yet, when Jesus heard that Lazarus was sick, he stayed where he was for two more days." 
John11:7 Then, after the two days, Jesus said to his disciples, ""Let's go back to Judea.""" 
John11:7 Then, after the two days, Jesus said to his disciples, ""Let's go back to Judea.""" 
John11:8 The disciples said to him, ""Rabbi, not long ago the Jews wanted to stone you to death. Do you really want to go back there?""" 
John11:9 Jesus answered, ""Aren't there twelve hours of daylight? Those who walk during the day don't stumble, because they see the light of this world."
